1. import { defineCollection, reference } from 'astro:content';
2. import { glob, file } from 'astro/loaders';
3. import { z } from 'astro/zod';
4. 
5. // ========================
6. // PRESENTATIONS COLLECTION
7. // ========================
8. const presentations = defineCollection({
9.     loader: glob({ pattern: "**/*.md", base: "./src/content/presentations" }),
10.     schema: z.object({
11.         title: z.string(),
12.         slug: z.string().optional(),
13.         description: z.string(),
14.         type: z.enum([
15.             'session',
16.             'workshop',
17.             'lightning-talk',
18.             'keynote',
19.             'panel',
20.             'webinar'
21.         ]).default('session'),
22.         // Support multiple presentation lengths (e.g., 45, 60, 75 minute versions)
23.         durations: z.array(z.number()).optional(),
24.         level: z.enum(['introductory', 'intermediate', 'advanced', 'all']).default('all'),
25. 
26.         // Learning objectives
27.         learningObjectives: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
28. 
29.         // Categorization
30.         tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
31. 
32.         // Related presentations
33.         relatedPresentations: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
34. 
35.         // Resources
36.         resources: z.array(z.object({
37.             type: z.enum(['slides', 'video', 'github', 'code', 'demo', 'blog', 'download', 'documentation', 'other']),
38.             title: z.string(),
39.             url: z.string(),
40.             description: z.string().optional(),
41.         })).optional(),
42. 
43.         // Media
44.         heroImage: z.string().optional(),
45. 
46.         // Status
47.         status: z.enum(['active', 'retired', 'in-development']).default('active'),
48.         featured: z.boolean().default(false),
49.     }),
50. });
51. 
52. // ========================
53. // SPEAKING EVENTS COLLECTION
54. // ========================
55. const events = defineCollection({
56.     loader: glob({ pattern: "**/*.md", base: "./src/content/events" }),
57.     schema: z.object({
58.         title: z.string(),
59.         slug: z.string().optional(),
60.         eventType: z.enum([
61.             'conference',
62.             'meetup',
63.             'webinar',
64.             'podcast',
65.             'workshop',
66.             'user-group',
67.             'corporate',
68.             'other'
69.         ]).default('conference'),
70.         description: z.string().optional(),
71. 
72.         // Event details
73.         startDate: z.coerce.date(),
74.         endDate: z.coerce.date().optional(),
75.         location: z.object({
76.             venue: z.string().optional(),
77.             city: z.string(),
78.             state: z.string().optional(),
79.             country: z.string(),
80.         }),
81. 
82.         // Linked presentation(s)
83.         presentations: z.array(z.union([
84.             z.string(), // Simple presentation ID for backward compatibility
85.             z.object({
86.                 id: z.string(),
87.                 sessionName: z.string().optional(), // Custom session name for this event
88.                 date: z.string().optional(), // e.g., "February 27, 2026"
89.                 time: z.string().optional(), // e.g., "10:00 AM" or "10:00 AM - 11:00 AM"
90.                 timeZone: z.string().optional(), // e.g., "EST", "PST", "UTC"
91.                 room: z.string().optional(), // e.g., "ST-Laurent 6"
92.                 sessionUrl: z.string().url().optional(), // Link to the session on event website
93.             })
94.         ])).default([]),
95. 
96.         // Links

101.

<summary>29 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. title: "Embracing Azure Container Apps for Effortless, Sustainable Containerization"
3. slug: "embracing-azure-container-apps"
4. description: "Discover how Azure Container Apps deliver enterprise-grade containerization with effortless operations, built-in sustainability, and real-world strategies that eliminate Kubernetes complexity and unlock long-term operational success."
5. type: "session"
6. level: "introductory"
7. durations: [45, 60, 75]
8. tags: ["Architecture", "Azure", "Cloud Architecture", "Cloud Development", "Cloud-Native", "Scalability"]
9. featured: false
10. heroImage: "/images/presentations/embracing-azure-container-apps.webp"
11. 
12. learningObjectives:
13.   - "**Simplify Container Orchestration Without Kubernetes Overhead**: Understand how Azure Container Apps provide essential containerization capabilities while eliminating the complexity and management challenges inherent in Kubernetes environments."
14.   - "**Achieve Effortless and Sustainable Container Management**: Discover how to leverage automatic scaling, integrated services, and robust security to optimize resource usage, reduce costs, and support long-term, sustainable container strategies."
15.   - "**Transform Your Container Strategy with Real-World Insights**: Gain practical, actionable knowledge from real-world case studies and best practices that showcase streamlined container deployments and efficient, scalable application management."
16. 
17. resources:
18.   - title: "Presentation Slides"
19.     url: "https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/EmbracingAzureContainerApps"
20.     type: "slides"
21.   - title: "GitHub Repository"
22.     url: "https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/EmbracingAzureContainerApps"
23.     type: "code"
24.   - title: "Demonstration"
25.     url: "https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/EmbracingAzureContainerApps"
26.     type: "documentation"
27. ---
28. 
29. As containerization becomes a cornerstone of modern application development, organizations seek solutions that deliver enterprise-grade capabilities without the operational complexities of Kubernetes. In this session, discover how Azure Container Apps offer a robust, simplified alternative providing powerful container management without the overhead of Kubernetes. Experience how built-in features like automatic scaling, integrated services, and enhanced security pave the way for effortless, sustainable deployments. Through real-world case studies and best practices, learn how to optimize resource usage, reduce costs, and streamline container orchestration, setting the stage for long-term operational success.
```

</details>

1. ---
2. title: "Generative AI for .NET Developers"
3. description: "Learn to create intelligent .NET applications with Azure OpenAI and ChatGPT. Discover generative AI concepts, best practices, and see a live demo of AI building AI apps."
4. type: "session"

36. 
37. ## Generative AI for .NET Developers
38. 
39. ### Elevator Pitch
40. 
41. Learn to create intelligent .NET apps with Azure OpenAI! Discover how to leverage ChatGPT, understand AI concepts, and see a live demo of AI building AI apps.
42. 
43. ### Short Abstract
44. 
45. Discover how to use .NET, C#, and Azure to create amazing applications with OpenAI and other generative AI technologies. You will learn the fundamental concepts of this latest wave of AI, the benefits and challenges of using .NET with it, and the best practices and tips for developing intelligent apps. You will also see a live demo of how AI can build AI apps using .NET and OpenAI.
46. 
47. ### Full Abstract
48. 
49. Have you ever wondered how to create intelligent apps with .NET and Azure OpenAI? Do you want to learn how to leverage the power of large language models like ChatGPT to generate natural and engaging conversations? Do you want to see how AI can build AI apps? If you answered yes to these questions, this talk is for you!
50. 
51. In this talk, you will discover how to use .NET, C#, and Azure to create amazing applications with OpenAI and other generative AI technologies. You will learn the fundamental concepts of this latest wave of AI, the benefits and challenges of using .NET with it, and the best practices and tips for developing intelligent apps. You will also see a live demo of how AI can build AI apps using .NET and OpenAI.
52. 
53. ### Presentation Type
54. 
55. - 45-minute session
56. - 60-minute session
57. - 75-minute session
58. 
59. ### Target Audience
60. 
61. - .NET developers interested in AI and machine learning
62. - Developers looking to integrate generative AI into their applications
63. - Teams exploring Azure OpenAI capabilities
64. - Anyone curious about practical applications of ChatGPT and large language models
65.
```

successfully downloaded text file (SHA: d974dd96b7174d848ac5ffc84013e1960154767d)![Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution](thumbnail.jpg)

## Short Abstract
Hands-on full-day workshop where you will design, develop, and publish a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution.

## Abstract
You have heard all the buzzwords such as microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, etc. You probably have attended sessions that talk about these terms. But how do you put all that together?

During this full-day workshop, you will start by designing a solution using serverless and event-driven cloud services using microservice patterns. Then you will build that solution using .NET, Azure services, and other best-practice tools. Finally, you will deploy that solution to the cloud so your customers can reap the rewards of a well-architected, reliable, and scalable solution that meets their needs today and provides for growth in the future.

# Type
- Full-Day Workshop

## Tags
- .NET
- APIs
- Application Development
- Architecture
- Azure
- Azure Event Hubs
- Azure Functions
- Azure Service Bus
- C#
- Cloud
- Cloud Architecture
- Cloud Computing
- Cloud Development
- Cloud Integration
- Cloud Resources
- Cloud Services
- Cloud Solutions
- Cloud-Native
- Cloud-Native Services
- Event Processing
- Event-driven
- Event-Driven Architecture
- Event-Driven Solutions
- Hands-on Training
- Hands-on Workshop
- Microservices
- Microsoft
- Microsoft Azure
- Microservices
- Performance Optimization
- Real-Time Event Processing
- Reliability
- REST
- Scalability
- Scalable Architectures
- Security
- Serverless
- Serverless Computing
- Software Design
- Software Development
- Technology
- Training
- Workshop

## Learning Objectives
- Understand the different serverless, event-driven cloud services and how to put them together to build a cohesive microservice-based architecture
- Build a working serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using best practices based upon real-world experience of what has worked and what has not
- Get hands-on experience deploying the solution you have built using continuous integration/continuous development tools that make it easy to keep your solution updated with the latest changes

## Presentations

Email [chadgreen@chadgreen.com](mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com?subject=Presentation%20Request:%20Presentation%20Title) to have Chad present this session at your event.

## Resources

<summary>35 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. title: "Advanced Serverless Workshop"
3. slug: "advanced-serverless-workshop"
4. description: "An immersive full-day hands-on workshop exploring advanced serverless architectures across leading cloud platforms: Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Amazon Web Services (AWS), and Microsoft Azure."
5. type: "workshop"
6. level: "advanced"
7. durations: [420, 480, 540]
8. tags: ["Serverless", "Cloud Architecture", "AWS", "Azure", "GCP", "Hands-On", "Best Practices", "Cloud Computing", "Architecture Patterns"]
9. featured: true
10. heroImage: "/images/presentations/advanced-serverless-workshop.webp"
11. status: "active"
12. 
13. learningObjectives:
14.   - "**Master Serverless Fundamentals:** Gain a comprehensive understanding of serverless computing principles, architecture patterns, and how to evaluate serverless solutions for real-world business problems."
15.   - "**Multi-Cloud Serverless Implementation:** Learn platform-specific solutions and best practices for implementing serverless architectures across AWS, Azure, and GCP."
16.   - "**Design Production-Ready Solutions:** Develop practical skills to design, implement, and deploy serverless solutions that are scalable, resilient, and cost-effective."
17.   - "**Hands-On Practical Experience:** Build real-world serverless applications through guided exercises and labs that reinforce key concepts and best practices."
18. ---
19. 
20. Elevate your serverless architecture expertise in this comprehensive full-day workshop. Dive deep into advanced serverless computing with hands-on experiences as we explore practical implementations, best practices, and platform-specific solutions across Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Amazon Web Services (AWS), and Microsoft Azure.
21. 
22. Led by expert practitioners, this immersive session offers invaluable insights to help you:
23. 
24. - **Understand Core Concepts:** Master the principles of serverless computing and when to apply them in your solutions.
25. 
26. - **Explore Platform Solutions:** Learn platform-specific approaches on AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, Google Cloud Functions, and related services.
27. 
28. - **Implement Best Practices:** Discover proven patterns for authentication, state management, error handling, and monitoring in serverless environments.
29. 
30. - **Build Real-World Applications:** Get hands-on experience building scalable, production-ready serverless solutions.
31. 
32. - **Optimize for Cost and Performance:** Learn strategies to optimize serverless workloads for both cost and performance.
33. 
34. This workshop is ideal for architects, senior developers, and DevOps engineers who want to master advanced serverless patterns and bring multi-cloud serverless expertise to their organizations. By the end of the day, you'll have the knowledge and practical experience to architect and implement sophisticated serverless solutions.
35.
```

</details>

36. 
37. ## Generative AI for .NET Developers
38. 
39. ### Elevator Pitch
40. 
41. Learn to create intelligent .NET apps with Azure OpenAI! Discover how to leverage ChatGPT, understand AI concepts, and see a live demo of AI building AI apps.
42. 
43. ### Short Abstract
44. 
45. Discover how to use .NET, C#, and Azure to create amazing applications with OpenAI and other generative AI technologies. You will learn the fundamental concepts of this latest wave of AI, the benefits and challenges of using .NET with it, and the best practices and tips for developing intelligent apps. You will also see a live demo of how AI can build AI apps using .NET and OpenAI.
46. 
47. ### Full Abstract
48. 
49. Have you ever wondered how to create intelligent apps with .NET and Azure OpenAI? Do you want to learn how to leverage the power of large language models like ChatGPT to generate natural and engaging conversations? Do you want to see how AI can build AI apps? If you answered yes to these questions, this talk is for you!
50. 
51. In this talk, you will discover how to use .NET, C#, and Azure to create amazing applications with OpenAI and other generative AI technologies. You will learn the fundamental concepts of this latest wave of AI, the benefits and challenges of using .NET with it, and the best practices and tips for developing intelligent apps. You will also see a live demo of how AI can build AI apps using .NET and OpenAI.
52. 
53. ### Presentation Type
54. 
55. - 45-minute session
56. - 60-minute session
57. - 75-minute session
58. 
59. ### Target Audience
60. 
61. - .NET developers interested in AI and machine learning
62. - Developers looking to integrate generative AI into their applications
63. - Teams exploring Azure OpenAI capabilities
64. - Anyone curious about practical applications of ChatGPT and large language models
65.
```

</details>

24. 
25. ## About Beer City Code
26. 
27. Beer City Code is a community-driven developer conference held in Grand Rapids, Michigan, known for its welcoming atmosphere and commitment to fostering local talent and community involvement. The conference brings together software developers, architects, and technology professionals for a day of learning, networking, and professional development.
28. 
29. With sessions covering modern development practices, cloud architecture, software design, and emerging technologies, Beer City Code offers valuable insights for developers at all levels of experience. The conference is known for its emphasis on community engagement and supporting local speakers.
30. 
31. ## Event Details
32. 
33. **Date:** August 2-3, 2024  
34. **Location:** Calvin University, Fine Arts Center, Grand Rapids, MI
35. 
36. ## Community Celebration
37. 
38. Beer City Code celebrates the vibrant developer community in Grand Rapids and the Midwest. By bringing together local and regional speakers, the conference creates opportunities for knowledge sharing, networking, and community building within the software development profession.
39. 
40.

21. ## About CodeMash
22. 
23. CodeMash is one of the largest community-driven technology conferences in North America. Held annually at the Kalahari Resorts in Sandusky, Ohio, CodeMash brings together software developers, architects, and technology professionals for two days of learning, networking, and professional development.
24. 
25. The conference features a diverse range of sessions covering topics from cloud architecture and microservices to security, AI, and software craftsmanship. Whether you're a seasoned architect or an early-career developer, CodeMash offers valuable insights and networking opportunities.
26. 
27. ## Event Details
28. 
29. **Dates:** January 16-17, 2025  
30. **Location:** [Kalahari Resorts & Conventions](https://www.kalahariresorts.com/sandusky), Sandusky, OH
31. 
32. ## Community Driven
33. 
34. CodeMash is built on community participation, with speakers ranging from local professionals to industry experts. The conference emphasizes practical, real-world knowledge sharing and creates an environment where attendees can learn from their peers, make connections, and grow professionally.
35.

<details>
<summary>25 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. title: "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution"
3. description: "A hands-on full-day workshop where you design, develop, and deploy a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using .NET, Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs."
4. type: "workshop"
5. durations: [480]
6. level: "intermediate"
7. tags:
8.   - "event-driven"
9.   - "eda"
10.   - "microservices"
11.   - "messaging"
12.   - "workshop"
13.   - "architecture"
14.   - "azure"
15.   - "serverless"
16.   - "dotnet"
17. heroImage: "/images/presentations/eda-microservices-workshop-v1.webp"
18. status: "active"
19. featured: false
20. 
21. learningObjectives:
22.   - "**Understand Serverless and Event-Driven Services**: Explore the Azure services that underpin serverless event-driven architectures—Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs—and understand how each fits into a cohesive microservice design."
23.   - "**Design a Microservice-Based Architecture**: Work through the architecture phase of a real solution, applying microservice patterns including bounded contexts, independent deployability, and loose coupling via event-driven messaging."
24.   - "**Implement Event-Driven Communication**: Build producer and consumer services that communicate asynchronously through Azure Service Bus queues/topics and Azure Event Hubs, eliminating tight inter-service dependencies."
25.   - "**Build Serverless Microservices with Azure Functions**: Develop individual microservices as Azure Functions, leveraging trigger bindings (HTTP, Service Bus, Event Hub) and output bindings to reduce boilerplate and simplify integration."
```

</details>

1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import PageHeader from '../../components/ui/PageHeader.astro';
4. import EventCard from '../../components/cards/EventCard.astro';
5. import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
6. 
7. // Get all events
8. let allEvents: any[] = [];
9. try {
10.     allEvents = await getCollection('events');
11. } catch (e) {
12.     // Collection might not exist yet
13. }
14. 
15. // Separate upcoming and past events
16. const now = new Date();
17. const upcomingEvents = allEvents
18.     .filter(e => e.data.startDate >= now)
19.     .sort((a, b) => a.data.startDate.getTime() - b.data.startDate.getTime());
20. 
21. const pastEvents = allEvents
22.     .filter(e => e.data.startDate < now)
23.     .sort((a, b) => b.data.startDate.getTime() - a.data.startDate.getTime());
24. 
25. // Group past events by year
26. const pastEventsByYear = pastEvents.reduce((acc, event) => {
27.     const year = event.data.startDate.getFullYear();
28.     if (!acc[year]) acc[year] = [];
29.     acc[year].push(event);
30.     return acc;
31. }, {} as Record<number, any[]>);
32. 
33. const years = Object.keys(pastEventsByYear).sort((a, b) => Number(b) - Number(a));
34. 
35. function formatDateRange(start: Date, end?: Date): string {
36.     const utc = { timeZone: 'UTC' as const };
37.     if (!end || start.getTime() === end.getTime()) {
38.         return start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric', ...utc });
39.     }
40.     const sameMonth = start.getUTCMonth() === end.getUTCMonth() && start.getUTCFullYear() === end.getUTCFullYear();
41.     if (sameMonth) {
42.         return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.getUTCDate()}, ${end.getUTCFullYear()}`;
43.     }
44.     return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric', ...utc })}`;
45. }
46. 
47. function formatLocation(loc: any): string {
48.     if (loc.virtual) return 'Virtual';
49.     const parts = [loc.city];
50.     if (loc.state) parts.push(loc.state);
51.     parts.push(loc.country);
52.     return parts.join(', ');
53. }
54. ---
55. 
56. <BaseLayout 
57.     title="Speaking Engagements" 
58.     description="Upcoming and past speaking engagements by Chad Green at conferences, meetups, and events worldwide."
59. >
60.     <PageHeader title="Speaking Engagements">
61.         <p>Conferences, meetups, and events where I've spoken or will be speaking</p>
62.     </PageHeader>
63. 
64.     <!-- Upcoming Events -->
65.     <section class="section">
66.         <div class="container">
67.             <h2 class="section-title">Upcoming Events</h2>
68. 
69.             {upcomingEvents.length > 0 ? (
70.                 <div class="upcoming-grid grid grid-3">
71.                     {upcomingEvents.map((event) => (
72.                         <EventCard
73.                             title={event.data.title}
74.                             slug={event.id}
75.                             startDate={event.data.startDate}
76.                             endDate={event.data.endDate}
77.                             location={event.data.location}
78.                             heroImage={event.data.heroImage}
79.                             eventType={event.data.eventType}
80.                             status="upcoming"
81.                             eventUrl={event.data.eventUrl}
82.                             featured={event.data.featured}
83.                         />
84.                     ))}
85.                 </div>
86.             ) : (
87.                 <div class="empty-state">
88.                     <p>No upcoming events scheduled. Check back soon!</p>
89.                 </div>
90.             )}
91.         </div>
92.     </section>
93. 
94.     <!-- Past Events by Year -->
95.     {years.length > 0 && (
96.         <section class="section section-alt">
97.             <div class="container">
98.                 <h2 class="section-title">Past Events</h2>
99. 
100.                 {years.map((year) => (
101.                     <div class="year-section">
102.                         <h3 class="year-heading">{year}</h3>
103.                         <div class="events-table">
104.                             <table>
105.                                 <thead>
106.                                     <tr>
107.                                         <th>Date</th>
108.                                         <th>Event</th>
109.                                         <th>Location</th>
110.                                     </tr>
111.                                 </thead>
112.                                 <tbody>
113.                                     {pastEventsByYear[Number(year)].map((event: any) => (
114.                                         <tr>
115.                                             <td class="date-col">
116.                                                 {formatDateRange(event.data.startDate, event.data.endDate)}
117.                                             </td>
118.                                             <td class="event-col">
119.                                                 <a href={`/speaking/${event.id}`}>{event.data.title}</a>
120.                                             </td>
121.                                             <td class="location-col">
122.                                                 {formatLocation(event.data.location)}
123.                                             </td>
124.                                         </tr>
125.                                     ))}
126.                                 </tbody>
127.                             </table>
128.                         </div>
129.                     </div>
130.                 ))}
131.             </div>
132.         </section>
133.     )}
134. </BaseLayout>
135.

138.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-8);
139.     }
140.

<details>
<summary>267 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. import Badge from '../ui/Badge.astro';
3. import Button from '../ui/Button.astro';
4. 
5. export interface Props {
6.     title: string;
7.     slug: string;
8.     startDate: Date;
9.     endDate?: Date;
10.     location: {
11.         city: string;
12.         state?: string;
13.         country: string;
14.         virtual?: boolean;
15.     };
16.     logo?: string;
17.     thumbnail?: string;
18.     heroImage?: string;
19.     eventType?: string;
20.     status?: string;
21.     eventUrl?: string;
22.     slidesUrl?: string;
23.     demosUrl?: string;
24.     featured?: boolean;
25.     compact?: boolean;
26.     class?: string;
27. }
28. 
29. const {
30.     title,
31.     slug,
32.     startDate,
33.     endDate,
34.     location,
35.     logo,
36.     thumbnail,
37.     heroImage,
38.     eventType = 'conference',
39.     status = 'upcoming',
40.     eventUrl,
41.     slidesUrl,
42.     demosUrl,
43.     featured = false,
44.     compact = false,
45.     class: className = '',
46. } = Astro.props;
47. 
48. const utc = { timeZone: 'UTC' as const };
49. 
50. function formatDate(date: Date): string {
51.     return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
52.         month: 'short',
53.         day: 'numeric',
54.         year: 'numeric',
55.         ...utc,
56.     });
57. }
58. 
59. function formatDateRange(start: Date, end?: Date): string {
60.     if (!end || start.getTime() === end.getTime()) {
61.         return formatDate(start);
62.     }
63.     
64.     const sameMonth = start.getUTCMonth() === end.getUTCMonth() && start.getUTCFullYear() === end.getUTCFullYear();
65.     
66.     if (sameMonth) {
67.         return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.getUTCDate()}, ${end.getUTCFullYear()}`;
68.     }
69.     
70.     return `${formatDate(start)} - ${formatDate(end)}`;
71. }
72. 
73. function formatLocation(loc: typeof location): string {
74.     if (loc.virtual) return 'Virtual Event';
75.     const parts = [loc.city];
76.     if (loc.state) parts.push(loc.state);
77.     parts.push(loc.country);
78.     return parts.join(', ');
79. }
80. 
81. const isUpcoming = status === 'upcoming' || status === 'confirmed';
82. const displayImage = heroImage || logo || thumbnail || '/images/events/default-event.svg';
83. const primaryHref = `/speaking/${slug}`;
84. ---
85. 
86. <article class:list={['event-card', { 'featured': featured, 'compact': compact }, className]}>
87.     <a href={primaryHref} class="card-image-link" aria-label={`View details for ${title}`}>
88.         <div class="card-image">
89.             <img src={displayImage} alt={`${title} logo`} loading="lazy" />
90.             {isUpcoming && <span class="status-badge upcoming">Upcoming</span>}
91.             {status === 'completed' && <span class="status-badge completed">Past Event</span>}
92.         </div>
93.     </a>
94.     <div class="card-content">
95.         <div class="card-meta">
96.             <span class="event-date">{formatDateRange(startDate, endDate)}</span>
97.             {location.virtual && <Badge variant="secondary" size="sm">Virtual</Badge>}
98.         </div>
99.         <h3 class="card-title">
100.             <a href={primaryHref}>{title}</a>
101.         </h3>
102.         <p class="card-location">{formatLocation(location)}</p>
103.         
104.         {!compact && (slidesUrl || demosUrl) && (
105.             <div class="resource-links">
106.                 {slidesUrl && (
107.                     <a href={slidesUrl} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="resource-link">
108.                         📊 Slides
109.                     </a>
110.                 )}
111.                 {demosUrl && (
112.                     <a href={demosUrl} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="resource-link">
113.                         💻 Demos
114.                     </a>
115.                 )}
116.             </div>
117.         )}
118.         
119.         <div class="card-actions">
120.             <Button href={primaryHref} variant="primary" size="sm">
121.                 Details
122.             </Button>
123.             {eventUrl && (
124.                 <Button href={eventUrl} variant="outline" size="sm">
125.                     Event Site ↗
126.                 </Button>
127.             )}
128.         </div>
129.     </div>
130. </article>
131. 
132. <style>
133.     .event-card {
134.         background-color: var(--color-bg-primary);
135.         border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
136.         box-shadow: var(--shadow-card);
137.         overflow: hidden;
138.         display: flex;
139.         flex-direction: column;
140.         transition: all var(--transition-normal);
141.     }
142. 
143.     .event-card:hover {
144.         box-shadow: var(--shadow-card-hover);
145.         transform: translateY(-4px);

1. import type { CollectionEntry } from 'astro:content';
2. 
3. export type EngagementPresentationEntry = CollectionEntry<'engagementPresentations'>;
4. 
5. export function getSpeakingSessionPath(entry: EngagementPresentationEntry): string {
6.     return `/speaking/${entry.data.eventSlug}/presentations/${entry.data.sessionSlug}`;
7. }
8. 
9. export function getPresentationEventPath(entry: EngagementPresentationEntry): string {
10.     return `/presentations/${entry.data.presentationSlug}/events/${entry.data.eventSlug}`;
11. }
12. 
13. export function getCanonicalPath(entry: EngagementPresentationEntry): string {
14.     if (entry.data.canonicalPath === 'speaking-session') {
15.         return getSpeakingSessionPath(entry);
16.     }
17. 
18.     return getPresentationEventPath(entry);
19. }
20. 
21. export function getResourceIcon(type: string): string {
22.     const icons: Record<string, string> = {
23.         slides: '📊',
24.         video: '🎬',
25.         github: '💻',
26.         code: '⌨️',
27.         demo: '🚀',
28.         blog: '📝',
29.         download: '⬇️',
30.         documentation: '📚',
31.         lab: '🧪',
32.         other: '🔗',
33.     };
34. 
35.     return icons[type] || '🔗';
36. }
37.
```

1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import PageHeader from '../../components/ui/PageHeader.astro';
4. import PresentationCard from '../../components/cards/PresentationCard.astro';
5. import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
6. 
7. // Get all active presentations
8. let presentations: any[] = [];
9. try {
10.     const allPresentations = await getCollection('presentations');
11.     presentations = allPresentations
12.         .sort((a, b) => {
13.             // Featured first, then by creation date
14.             if (a.data.featured && !b.data.featured) return -1;
15.             if (!a.data.featured && b.data.featured) return 1;
16.             const dateA = a.data.createdDate?.getTime() || 0;
17.             const dateB = b.data.createdDate?.getTime() || 0;
18.             return dateB - dateA;
19.         });
20. } catch (e) {
21.     // Collection might not exist yet
22. }
23. 
24. // Group by type for filtering
25. const presentationTypes = [...new Set(presentations.map(p => p.data.type))];
26. ---
27. 
28. <BaseLayout 
29.     title="Presentations" 
30.     description="Browse all presentations by Chad Green on Azure, .NET, DevOps, and more."
31. >
32.     <PageHeader title="Presentations">
33.         <p>Explore my collection of talks, workshops, and presentations</p>
34.     </PageHeader>
35. 
36.     <section class="section">
37.         <div class="container">
38.             <!-- Filter Controls -->
39.             {presentationTypes.length > 1 && (
40.                 <div class="filter-controls">
41.                     <button class="filter-btn active" data-filter="all">All</button>
42.                     {presentationTypes.map((type) => (
43.                         <button class="filter-btn" data-filter={type}>
44.                             {type.replace('-', ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, l => l.toUpperCase())}
45.                         </button>
46.                     ))}
47.                 </div>
48.             )}
49. 
50.             {presentations.length > 0 ? (
51.                 <div class="presentations-grid grid grid-3" id="presentations-grid">
52.                     {presentations.map((presentation) => (
53.                         <div class="presentation-item" data-type={presentation.data.type}>
54.                             <PresentationCard
55.                                 title={presentation.data.title}
56.                                 description={presentation.data.description}
57.                                 slug={presentation.id}
58.                                 thumbnail={presentation.data.heroImage}
59.                                 presentationType={presentation.data.type}
60.                                 tags={presentation.data.tags}
61.                                 featured={presentation.data.featured}
62.                             />
63.                         </div>
64.                     ))}
65.                 </div>
66.             ) : (
67.                 <div class="empty-state">
68.                     <h3>No Presentations Yet</h3>
69.                     <p>Check back soon for my collection of presentations.</p>
70.                 </div>
71.             )}
72.         </div>
73.     </section>
74. 
75.     <!-- Book CTA -->
76.     <section class="book-cta">
77.         <div class="container">
78.             <div class="cta-content">
79.                 <p>
80.                     <strong>Interested in one of these presentations?</strong><br>
81.                     Email <a href="mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com">chadgreen@chadgreen.com</a> to have Chad present at your event.
82.                 </p>
83.             </div>
84.         </div>
85.     </section>
86. </BaseLayout>
87. 
88. <style>
89.     .filter-controls {
90.         display: flex;
91.         flex-wrap: wrap;
92.         gap: var(--spacing-2);
93.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-8);
94.         justify-content: center;
95.     }
96. 
97.     .filter-btn {
98.         padding: var(--spacing-2) var(--spacing-4);
99.         background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
100.         border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
101.         border-radius: var(--radius-full);
102.         font-size: var(--font-size-sm);
103.         font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium);
104.         color: var(--color-text-secondary);
105.         cursor: pointer;
106.         transition: all var(--transition-fast);
107.     }
108. 
109.     .filter-btn:hover {
110.         border-color: var(--color-primary);
111.         color: var(--color-primary);
112.     }
113. 
114.     .filter-btn.active {
115.         background-color: var(--color-primary);
116.         border-color: var(--color-primary);
117.         color: var(--color-text-inverse);
118.     }
119. 
120.     .presentation-item {
121.         transition: opacity var(--transition-fast);
122.     }
123. 
124.     .presentation-item.hidden {
125.         display: none;
126.     }
127. 
128.     .empty-state {
129.         text-align: center;
130.         padding: var(--spacing-16);
131.         background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
132.         border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
133.     }
134. 
135.     .empty-state h3 {
136.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-2);
137.     }
138. 
139.     .empty-state p {
140.         color: var(--color-text-secondary);
141.         margin-bottom: 0;
142.     }
143. 
144.     .book-cta {
145.         background-color: var(--color-primary-bg);
146.         padding: var(--spacing-8) 0;
147.     }
148. 
149.     .book-cta .cta-content {
150.         text-align: center;
151.     }
152. 
153.     .book-cta a {
154.         color: var(--color-primary);
155.         font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold);
156.     }
157. </style>
158. 
159. <script>
160.     const filterBtns = document.querySelectorAll('.filter-btn');
161.     const items = document.querySelectorAll('.presentation-item');
162. 
163.     filterBtns.forEach(btn => {
164.         btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
165.             const filter = (btn as HTMLElement).dataset.filter;
166. 
167.             // Update active state
168.             filterBtns.forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
169.             btn.classList.add('active');
170. 
171.             // Filter items
172.             items.forEach(item => {
173.                 const type = (item as HTMLElement).dataset.type;
174.                 if (filter === 'all' || type === filter) {
175.                     item.classList.remove('hidden');
176.                 } else {
177.                     item.classList.add('hidden');
178.                 }
179.             });
180.         });
181.     });
182. </script>
183.
```

2. import Button from '../ui/Button.astro';
3. 
4. export interface Props {
5.     title: string;
6.     date: Date;
7.     shortDescription: string;
8.     eventUrl?: string;
9.     thumbnail?: string;
10.     class?: string;
11.     meetupGroup?: string; // Meetup group slug
12.     eventSlug?: string; // Event slug for internal link
13. }
14. 
15. const {
16.     title,
17.     date,
18.     shortDescription,
19.     eventUrl,
20.     thumbnail,
21.     class: className = '',
22.     meetupGroup,
23.     eventSlug,
24. } = Astro.props;
25. 
26. // Internal event page takes precedence over external URL
27. const linkUrl = (meetupGroup && eventSlug) 
28.     ? `/meetups/${meetupGroup}/events/${eventSlug}` 
29.     : eventUrl;
30. 
31. function formatDate(date: Date): string {
32.     return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
33.         weekday: 'short',
34.         month: 'short',
35.         day: 'numeric',
36.         year: 'numeric',
37.         timeZone: 'UTC',
38.     });
39. }

44.         <a
45.             href={linkUrl}
46.             class="card-image-link"
47.             aria-label={`View details for ${title}`}
48.             target={eventUrl && !meetupGroup ? "_blank" : undefined}
49.             rel={eventUrl && !meetupGroup ? "noopener noreferrer" : undefined}
50.         >
51.             <div class="card-image">

53.             </div>
54.         </a>

65.                     size="sm"
66.                     target={eventUrl && !meetupGroup ? "_blank" : undefined}
67.                     rel={eventUrl && !meetupGroup ? "noopener noreferrer" : undefined}
68.                 >

1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
4. 
5. export async function getStaticPaths() {
6.     const presentations = await getCollection('presentations');
7. 
8.     return presentations.map((presentation) => ({
9.         params: { slug: presentation.id },
10.         props: {
11.             targetPath: `/presentations/${presentation.id}?tab=events`,
12.             presentationTitle: presentation.data.title,
13.         },
14.     }));
15. }
16. 
17. const { targetPath, presentationTitle } = Astro.props;
18. ---
19. 
20. <BaseLayout
21.     title={`${presentationTitle} Events`}
22.     description={`Redirecting to the events tab for ${presentationTitle}.`}
23.     canonicalUrl={new URL(targetPath, Astro.url.origin).toString()}
24. >
25.     <section class="section">
26.         <div class="container redirect-page">
27.             <h1>Redirecting...</h1>
28.             <p>If you are not redirected automatically, continue to <a id="redirect-link" href={targetPath}>events for this presentation</a>.</p>
29.         </div>
30.     </section>
31. 
32.     <script is:inline>
33.         const redirectLink = document.getElementById('redirect-link');
34.         if (redirectLink instanceof HTMLAnchorElement) {
35.             window.location.replace(redirectLink.href);
36.         }
37.     </script>
38. </BaseLayout>
39. 
40. <style>
41.     .redirect-page {
42.         text-align: center;
43.         max-width: 760px;
44.     }
45. </style>
46.
```

</details>

1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import Badge from '../../components/ui/Badge.astro';
4. import Button from '../../components/ui/Button.astro';
5. import TabNav from '../../components/ui/TabNav.astro';
6. import TabSyncScript from '../../components/ui/TabSyncScript.astro';
7. import PresentationCard from '../../components/cards/PresentationCard.astro';
8. import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content';
9. 
10. export async function getStaticPaths() {
11.     const events = await getCollection('events');
12.     return events.map((event) => ({
13.         params: { slug: event.id },
14.         props: { event },
15.     }));
16. }
17. 
18. const { event } = Astro.props;
19. const { Content } = await render(event);
20. 
21. // Get linked presentations with their event-specific details
22. let linkedPresentations: any[] = [];
23. if (event.data.presentations?.length) {
24.     const [allPresentations, allEngagementPresentations] = await Promise.all([
25.         getCollection('presentations'),
26.         getCollection('engagementPresentations'),
27.     ]);
28. 
29.     const sessionSlugByPresentationId = new Map(
30.         allEngagementPresentations
31.             .filter((entry) => entry.data.eventSlug === event.id)
32.             .map((entry) => [entry.data.presentationSlug, entry.data.sessionSlug])
33.     );
34.     
35.     linkedPresentations = event.data.presentations.map((p: any) => {
36.         // Handle both string format (old) and object format (new)
37.         const presentationId = typeof p === 'string' ? p : p.id;
38.         const presentationData = allPresentations.find(pres => pres.id === presentationId);
39.         
40.         if (!presentationData) return null;
41.         
42.         // Add event-specific scheduling details
43.         const sessionSlug = sessionSlugByPresentationId.get(presentationId);
44. 
45.         return {
46.             ...presentationData,
47.             detailsHref: sessionSlug
48.                 ? `/speaking/${event.id}/presentations/${sessionSlug}`
49.                 : `/presentations/${presentationId}`,
50.             sessionName: typeof p === 'object' ? p.sessionName : undefined,
51.             scheduleDate: typeof p === 'object' ? p.date : undefined,
52.             scheduleTime: typeof p === 'object' ? p.time : undefined,
53.             scheduleTimeZone: typeof p === 'object' ? p.timeZone : undefined,
54.             scheduleRoom: typeof p === 'object' ? p.room : undefined,
55.             sessionUrl: typeof p === 'object' ? p.sessionUrl : undefined,
56.         };
57.     }).filter(Boolean);
58. }
59. 
60. function formatDateRange(start: Date, end?: Date): string {
61.     const utc = { timeZone: 'UTC' as const };
62.     if (!end || start.getTime() === end.getTime()) {
63.         return start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { 
64.             weekday: 'long',
65.             month: 'long', 
66.             day: 'numeric', 
67.             year: 'numeric',
68.             ...utc
69.         });
70.     }
71.     const sameMonth = start.getUTCMonth() === end.getUTCMonth() && start.getUTCFullYear() === end.getUTCFullYear();
72.     if (sameMonth) {
73.         return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'long', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.getUTCDate()}, ${end.getUTCFullYear()}`;
74.     }
75.     return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'long', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'long', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric', ...utc })}`;
76. }
77. 
78. function formatLocation(loc: any): string {
79.     if (loc.virtual) return 'Virtual Event';
80.     const parts = [];
81.     if (loc.venue) parts.push(loc.venue);
82.     parts.push(loc.city);
83.     if (loc.state) parts.push(loc.state);
84.     parts.push(loc.country);
85.     return parts.join(', ');
86. }
87. 
88. // Determine if event is upcoming based on start date
89. const now = new Date();
90. const isUpcoming = event.data.startDate >= now;
91. 
92. const tabs = [
93.     { id: 'about', label: 'About' },
94.     { id: 'presentations', label: `My Presentations (${linkedPresentations.length})` },
95. ];
96. 
97. // Get current tab from URL
98. const currentTab = Astro.url.searchParams.get('tab') || 'about';
99. 
100. const typeLabels: Record<string, string> = {
101.     'conference': 'Conference',
102.     'meetup': 'Meetup',
103.     'webinar': 'Webinar',
104.     'podcast': 'Podcast',
105.     'workshop': 'Workshop',
106.     'user-group': 'User Group',
107.     'corporate': 'Corporate Event',
108.     'other': 'Event',
109. };
110. ---
111. 
112. <BaseLayout 
113.     title={event.data.title}
114.     description={event.data.description || `Speaking engagement at ${event.data.title}`}
115.     ogImage={event.data.heroImage}
116. >
117.     <section class="event-header">
118.         <div class="container">
119.             <div class="header-content">
120.                 <div class="event-logo">
121.                     <img 
122.                         src={event.data.heroImage || '/images/events/default-event.svg'} 
123.                         alt={`${event.data.title} logo`}
124.                     />
125.                 </div>
126.                 <div class="event-info">
127.                     <div class="event-meta">
128.                         {isUpcoming ? (
129.                             <Badge variant="primary">Upcoming</Badge>
130.                         ) : (
131.                             <Badge variant="default">Past Event</Badge>
132.                         )}
133.                         <Badge variant="secondary">
134.                             {typeLabels[event.data.eventType] || event.data.eventType}
135.                         </Badge>
136.                         {event.data.location.virtual && <Badge variant="outline">Virtual</Badge>}
137.                     </div>
138.                     
139.                     <h1 class="event-title">{event.data.title}</h1>
140.                     
141.                     {event.data.description && (
142.                         <p class="event-subtitle">{event.data.description}</p>
143.                     )}
144.                     
145.                     <div class="event-details">
146.                         <div class="detail-item">
147.                             <span class="detail-icon">📅</span>
148.                             <span class="detail-text">{formatDateRange(event.data.startDate, event.data.endDate)}</span>
149.                         </div>
150.                         <div class="detail-item">
151.                             <span class="detail-icon">📍</span>
152.                             <span class="detail-text">{formatLocation(event.data.location)}</span>
153.                         </div>
154.                         {event.data.audience && (
155.                             <div class="detail-item">
156.                                 <span class="detail-icon">👥</span>
157.                                 <span class="detail-text">{event.data.audience} attendees</span>
158.                             </div>
159.                         )}
160.                     </div>
161.                 </div>
162.             </div>
163.         </div>
164.     </section>
165. 
166.     <!-- Tab Navigation -->
167.     <div class="tabs-container">
168.         <div class="container">
169.             <TabNav 
170.                 tabs={tabs.map(t => ({ ...t, href: `?tab=${t.id}` }))} 
171.                 activeTab={currentTab} 
172.             />
173.         </div>
174.     </div>
175. 
176.     <!-- Tab Content -->
177.     <section class="section tab-content">
178.         <div class="container">
179.             <!-- About Tab -->
180.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="about" style={currentTab !== 'about' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
181.                 <div class="prose">
182.                     <Content />
183.                 </div>
184.                 
185.                 {event.data.website && (
186.                     <div class="event-links">
187.                         <Button href={event.data.website} variant="primary">
188.                             Visit Event Website ↗
189.                         </Button>
190.                     </div>
191.                 )}
192. 
193.                 {event.data.photos && event.data.photos.length > 0 && (
194.                     <div class="photos-section">
195.                         <h2>Photos</h2>
196.                         <div class="photos-grid">
197.                             {event.data.photos.map((photo: string) => (
198.                                 <div class="photo-item">
199.                                     <img src={photo} alt="Event photo" loading="lazy" />
200.                                 </div>
201.                             ))}
202.                         </div>
203.                     </div>
204.                 )}
205.             </div>
206. 
207.             <!-- Presentations Tab -->
208.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="presentations" style={currentTab !== 'presentations' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
209.                 {linkedPresentations.length > 0 ? (
210.                     <div class="presentations-list">
211.                         <h2>My Sessions at This Event</h2>
212.                         <div class="grid grid-3">
213.                             {linkedPresentations.map((presentation) => (
214.                                 <PresentationCard
215.                                     title={presentation.data.title}
216.                                     description={presentation.data.description}
217.                                     slug={presentation.id}
218.                                     detailsHref={presentation.detailsHref}
219.                                     thumbnail={presentation.data.heroImage}
220.                                     presentationType={presentation.data.type}
221.                                     tags={presentation.data.tags}
222.                                     showSchedule={true}
223.                                     sessionName={presentation.sessionName}
224.                                     scheduleDate={presentation.scheduleDate}
225.                                     scheduleTime={presentation.scheduleTime}
226.                                     scheduleTimeZone={presentation.scheduleTimeZone}
227.                                     scheduleRoom={presentation.scheduleRoom}
228.                                     sessionUrl={presentation.sessionUrl}
229.                                 />
230.                             ))}
231.                         </div>
232.                     </div>
233.                 ) : (
234.                     <div class="empty-state">
235.                         <p>No presentations listed for this event yet.</p>
236.                     </div>
237.                 )}
238.             </div>
239.         </div>
240.     </section>
241. 
242.     <TabSyncScript />
243. </BaseLayout>

292.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
293.     }

296.         display: flex;
297.         align-items: center;

309.     }

312.         display: flex;

318.         background-color: var(--color-bg-primary);

320.         position: sticky;
321.         top: 0;
322.         z-index: 10;
323.     }

326.         padding: var(--spacing-10) 0;
327.     }
328. 
329.     .tab-panel {
330.         animation: fadeIn 0.3s ease-in;
331.     }
332. 
333.     @keyframes fadeIn {
334.         from {
335.             opacity: 0;
336.             transform: translateY(10px);
337.         }
338.         to {
339.             opacity: 1;
340.             transform: translateY(0);
341.         }
342.     }
343.

345.         max-width: 800px;
346.         line-height: 1.7;
347.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-8);
348.     }
349.

351.         margin-top: var(--spacing-8);
352.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
353.     }
354.

357.     }
358.

360.         margin-top: var(--spacing-6);
361.     }
362.

364.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-6);
365.     }
366. 
367.     .empty-state {
368.         text-align: center;
369.         padding: var(--spacing-12) var(--spacing-4);
370.         color: var(--color-text-secondary);
371.     }
372. 
373.     .photos-section {
374.         margin-top: var(--spacing-10);
375.     }
376.

378.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-6);
379.     }
380. 
381.     .photos-grid {
382.         display: grid;
383.         grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr));
384.         gap: var(--spacing-4);
385.     }
386. 
387.     .photo-item {
388.         border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
389.         overflow: hidden;
390.         aspect-ratio: 16 / 10;
391.     }
392. 
393.     .photo-item img {
394.         width: 100%;
395.         height: 100%;
396.         object-fit: cover;
397.     }
398. 
399.     @media (max-width: 768px) {
400.         .header-content {
401.             grid-template-columns: 1fr;
402.         }
403. 
404.         .event-logo {
405.             max-width: 200px;
406.         }
407. 
408.         .event-title {
409.             font-size: var(--font-size-3xl);
410.         }
411.     }

413. 
414. <script>
415.     // Client-side tab switching
416.     document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
417.         const tabLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-item');
418.         const tabPanels = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel');
419.         
420.         tabLinks.forEach(link => {
421.             link.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
422.                 e.preventDefault();
423.                 const targetTab = link.getAttribute('data-tab');
424.                 
425.                 // Update active tab styling
426.                 tabLinks.forEach(l => l.classList.remove('tab-active'));
427.                 link.classList.add('tab-active');
428.                 
429.                 // Show/hide panels
430.                 tabPanels.forEach(panel => {
431.                     if (panel.getAttribute('data-tab') === targetTab) {
432.                         panel.style.display = '';
433.                     } else {
434.                         panel.style.display = 'none';
435.                     }
436.                 });
437.                 
438.                 // Update URL without reload
439.                 const url = new URL(window.location.href);
440.                 url.searchParams.set('tab', targetTab);
441.                 window.history.pushState({}, '', url);
442.             });
443.         });
444.     });
445. </script>
446.
```

</details>

1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import Badge from '../../components/ui/Badge.astro';
4. import Button from '../../components/ui/Button.astro';
5. import TabNav from '../../components/ui/TabNav.astro';
6. import TabSyncScript from '../../components/ui/TabSyncScript.astro';
7. import EventCard from '../../components/cards/EventCard.astro';
8. import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content';
9. import { marked } from 'marked';
10. 
11. export async function getStaticPaths() {
12.     const presentations = await getCollection('presentations');
13.     return presentations.map((presentation) => ({
14.         params: { slug: presentation.id },
15.         props: { presentation },
16.     }));
17. }
18. 
19. const { presentation } = Astro.props;
20. const { Content } = await render(presentation);
21. 
22. // Get related presentations
23. let relatedPresentations: any[] = [];
24. if (presentation.data.relatedPresentations?.length) {
25.     const allPresentations = await getCollection('presentations');
26.     relatedPresentations = allPresentations.filter(p => 
27.         presentation.data.relatedPresentations.includes(p.id)
28.     );
29. }
30. 
31. // Get events where this presentation was given
32. let presentationEvents: any[] = [];
33. try {
34.     const allEvents = await getCollection('events');
35.     presentationEvents = allEvents
36.         .filter(e => {
37.             if (!e.data.presentations) return false;
38.             // Handle both string format and object format
39.             return e.data.presentations.some((p: any) => {
40.                 const presentationId = typeof p === 'string' ? p : p.id;
41.                 return presentationId === presentation.id;
42.             });
43.         })
44.         .sort((a, b) => b.data.startDate.getTime() - a.data.startDate.getTime());
45. } catch (e) {
46.     // Collection might not exist yet
47. }
48. 
49. const tabs = [
50.     { id: 'abstract', label: 'Abstract' },
51.     { id: 'objectives', label: 'Learning Objectives' },
52.     { id: 'events', label: `Events (${presentationEvents.length})` },
53.     { id: 'resources', label: 'Resources' },
54. ];
55. 
56. // Get current tab from URL
57. const currentTab = Astro.url.searchParams.get('tab') || 'abstract';
58. 
59. const typeLabels: Record<string, string> = {
60.     'session': 'Session',
61.     'workshop': 'Workshop',
62.     'lightning-talk': 'Lightning Talk',
63.     'keynote': 'Keynote',
64.     'panel': 'Panel',
65.     'webinar': 'Webinar',
66. };
67. 
68. const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
69.     'active': 'Active',
70.     'retired': 'Retired',
71.     'in-development': 'In Development',
72. };
73. 
74. const statusVariants: Record<string, 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'outline' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger'> = {
75.     'active': 'success',
76.     'retired': 'outline',
77.     'in-development': 'warning',
78. };
79. ---
80. 
81. <BaseLayout 
82.     title={presentation.data.title}
83.     description={presentation.data.description}
84.     ogImage={presentation.data.heroImage}
85. >
86.     <!-- Presentation Header -->
87.     <section class="presentation-header">
88.         <div class="container">
89.             <div class="header-content">
90.                 <div class="header-image">
91.                     <img 
92.                         src={presentation.data.heroImage || '/images/presentations/default-thumbnail.svg'} 
93.                         alt="" 
94.                         loading="eager"
95.                     />
96.                 </div>
97.                 <div class="header-info">
98.                     <div class="header-meta">
99.                         <Badge variant="primary">{typeLabels[presentation.data.type] || presentation.data.type}</Badge>
100.                         {presentation.data.level && <Badge variant="secondary">{presentation.data.level}</Badge>}
101.                         <Badge variant={statusVariants[presentation.data.status]}>{statusLabels[presentation.data.status]}</Badge>
102.                         {presentation.data.durations && presentation.data.durations.length > 0 && (
103.                             <span class="duration">⏱ {presentation.data.durations.join(' / ')} min</span>
104.                         )}
105.                     </div>
106.                     
107.                     <h1 class="presentation-title">{presentation.data.title}</h1>
108.                     
109.                     <p class="presentation-description">{presentation.data.description}</p>
110.                     
111.                     {presentation.data.tags?.length > 0 && (
112.                         <div class="presentation-tags">
113.                             {presentation.data.tags.map((tag: string) => (
114.                                 <a href={`/presentations/tags/${encodeURIComponent(tag)}`} class="tag-link">
115.                                     <Badge variant="outline" size="sm">{tag}</Badge>
116.                                 </a>
117.                             ))}
118.                         </div>
119.                     )}
120. 
121.                     {relatedPresentations.length > 0 && (
122.                         <div class="related-presentations">
123.                             <span class="related-label">Related:</span>
124.                             {relatedPresentations.map((rp) => (
125.                                 <a href={`/presentations/${rp.id}`} class="related-link">{rp.data.shortTitle || rp.data.title}</a>
126.                             ))}
127.                         </div>
128.                     )}
129.                 </div>
130.             </div>
131.         </div>
132.     </section>
133. 
134.     <!-- Tab Navigation -->
135.     <div class="tabs-container">
136.         <div class="container">
137.             <TabNav 
138.                 tabs={tabs.map(t => ({ ...t, href: `?tab=${t.id}` }))} 
139.                 activeTab={currentTab} 
140.             />
141.         </div>
142.     </div>
143. 
144.     <!-- Tab Content -->
145.     <section class="section tab-content">
146.         <div class="container">
147.             <!-- Abstract Tab -->
148.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="abstract" style={currentTab !== 'abstract' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
149.                 <div class="prose">
150.                     {presentation.data.abstract ? (
151.                         <div set:html={presentation.data.abstract} />
152.                     ) : (
153.                         <Content />
154.                     )}
155.                 </div>
156.             </div>
157. 
158.             <!-- Learning Objectives Tab -->
159.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="objectives" style={currentTab !== 'objectives' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
160.                 {presentation.data.learningObjectives?.length > 0 ? (
161.                     <div class="objectives-list">
162.                         <h2>What You'll Learn</h2>
163.                         <ul>
164.                             {presentation.data.learningObjectives.map((objective: string, index: number) => (
165.                                 <li>
166.                                     <span class="objective-number">{index + 1}</span>
167.                                     <span class="objective-text" set:html={marked.parseInline(objective)} />
168.                                 </li>
169.                             ))}
170.                         </ul>
171.                     </div>
172.                 ) : (
173.                     <div class="empty-state">
174.                         <p>Learning objectives coming soon.</p>
175.                     </div>
176.                 )}
177.             </div>
178. 
179.             <!-- Events Tab -->
180.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="events" style={currentTab !== 'events' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
181.                 {presentationEvents.length > 0 ? (
182.                     <div class="events-list">
183.                         <h2>Where I've Presented This</h2>
184.                         <div class="grid grid-3">
185.                             {presentationEvents.map((event) => (
186.                                 <EventCard
187.                                     title={event.data.title}
188.                                     slug={event.id}
189.                                     startDate={event.data.startDate}
190.                                     endDate={event.data.endDate}
191.                                     location={event.data.location}
192.                                     heroImage={event.data.heroImage}
193.                                     logo={event.data.logo}
194.                                     thumbnail={event.data.thumbnail}
195.                                     eventType={event.data.eventType}
196.                                     status={event.data.status}
197.                                     eventUrl={event.data.eventUrl}
198.                                     slidesUrl={event.data.slidesUrl}
199.                                     demosUrl={event.data.demosUrl}
200.                                 />
201.                             ))}
202.                         </div>
203.                     </div>
204.                 ) : (
205.                     <div class="empty-state">
206.                         <p>No events scheduled yet for this presentation.</p>
207.                     </div>
208.                 )}
209.             </div>
210. 
211.             <!-- Resources Tab -->
212.             <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="resources" style={currentTab !== 'resources' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
213.                 {presentation.data.resources?.length > 0 ? (
214.                     <div class="resources-list">
215.                         <h2>Resources</h2>
216.                         <div class="resources-grid">
217.                             {presentation.data.resources.map((resource: any) => (
218.                                 <a href={resource.url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="resource-card">
219.                                     <span class="resource-icon">
220.                                         {resource.type === 'slides' && '📊'}
221.                                         {resource.type === 'video' && '🎬'}
222.                                         {resource.type === 'github' && '💻'}
223.                                         {resource.type === 'demo' && '🚀'}
224.                                         {resource.type === 'blog' && '📝'}
225.                                         {resource.type === 'download' && '⬇️'}
226.                                         {resource.type === 'other' && '🔗'}
227.                                     </span>
228.                                     <span class="resource-title">{resource.title}</span>
229.                                     {resource.description && <span class="resource-desc">{resource.description}</span>}
230.                                 </a>
231.                             ))}
232.                         </div>
233.                     </div>
234.                 ) : (
235.                     <div class="empty-state">
236.                         <p>Resources coming soon.</p>
237.                     </div>
238.                 )}
239.             </div>
240.         </div>
241.     </section>
242. 
243.     <!-- Book CTA -->
244.     <section class="book-cta">
245.         <div class="container">
246.             <div class="cta-content">
247.                 <p>
248.                     <strong>Want me to present this at your event?</strong><br>
249.                     Email <a href="mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com">chadgreen@chadgreen.com</a> to book this presentation.
250.                 </p>
251.                 <Button href="/contact?type=speaking" variant="primary">Contact Me</Button>
252.             </div>
253.         </div>
254.     </section>
255. 
256.     <TabSyncScript />
257. </BaseLayout>

360.     .events-list h2,
361.     .resources-list h2 {
362.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-6);
363.     }
364. 
365.     .objectives-list ul {
366.         list-style: none;
367.         padding: 0;
368.     }
369. 
370.     .objectives-list li {
371.         display: flex;
372.         align-items: flex-start;
373.         gap: var(--spacing-4);
374.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
375.         padding: var(--spacing-4);
376.         background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
377.         border-radius: var(--radius-md);
378.     }
379. 
380.     .objective-number {
381.         flex-shrink: 0;
382.         width: 32px;
383.         height: 32px;
384.         background-color: var(--color-primary);
385.         color: var(--color-text-inverse);
386.         border-radius: var(--radius-full);
387.         display: flex;
388.         align-items: center;
389.         justify-content: center;
390.         font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold);
391.     }
392. 
393.     .objective-text {
394.         font-size: var(--font-size-lg);
395.     }
396. 
397.     .resources-grid {
398.         display: grid;
399.         grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr));
400.         gap: var(--spacing-4);
401.     }
402. 
403.     .resource-card {
404.         display: flex;
405.         flex-direction: column;
406.         padding: var(--spacing-4);
407.         background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
408.         border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
409.         border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
410.         text-decoration: none;
411.         transition: all var(--transition-fast);
412.     }
413. 
414.     .resource-card:hover {
415.         border-color: var(--color-primary);
416.         transform: translateY(-2px);
417.     }
418. 
419.     .resource-icon {
420.         font-size: 2rem;
421.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-2);
422.     }
423. 
424.     .resource-title {
425.         font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold);
426.         color: var(--color-text-primary);
427.     }
428. 
429.     .resource-desc {
430.         font-size: var(--font-size-sm);
431.         color: var(--color-text-secondary);
432.         margin-top: var(--spacing-1);
433.     }
434. 
435.     .empty-state {
436.         text-align: center;
437.         padding: var(--spacing-12);
438.         background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
439.         border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
440.         color: var(--color-text-secondary);
441.     }
442. 
443.     .book-cta {
444.         background-color: var(--color-primary-bg);
445.         padding: var(--spacing-10) 0;
446.     }
447. 
448.     .cta-content {
449.         text-align: center;
450.         max-width: 600px;
451.         margin: 0 auto;
452.     }
453. 
454.     .cta-content p {
455.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
456.     }
457. 
458.     .cta-content a {
459.         color: var(--color-primary);
460.         font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold);
461.     }
462. 
463.     @media (max-width: 1024px) {
464.         .header-content {
465.             grid-template-columns: 1fr;
466.         }
467. 
468.         .header-image {
469.             max-width: 400px;
470.         }
471. 
472.         .presentation-title {
473.             font-size: var(--font-size-3xl);
474.         }
475.     }
476. </style>
477. 
478. <script>
479.     // Handle tab navigation without page reload
480.     const tabLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-nav a');
481.     const tabPanels = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel');
482. 
483.     tabLinks.forEach(link => {
484.         link.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
485.             e.preventDefault();
486.             const url = new URL((link as HTMLAnchorElement).href);
487.             const tabId = url.searchParams.get('tab');
488. 
489.             // Update URL without reload
490.             window.history.pushState({}, '', url);
491. 
492.             // Update active tab
493.             tabLinks.forEach(l => l.classList.remove('tab-active'));
494.             link.classList.add('tab-active');
495. 
496.             // Show corresponding panel
497.             tabPanels.forEach(panel => {
498.                 if ((panel as HTMLElement).dataset.tab === tabId) {
499.                     (panel as HTMLElement).style.display = 'block';
500.                 } else {
501.                     (panel as HTMLElement).style.display = 'none';
502.                 }
503.             });
504.         });
505.     });
506. </script>
507.
```

</details>
