```
1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import Badge from '../../../components/ui/Badge.astro';
4. import Button from '../../../components/ui/Button.astro';
5. import TabNav from '../../../components/ui/TabNav.astro';
6. import TabSyncScript from '../../../components/ui/TabSyncScript.astro';
7. import MeetupEventCard from '../../../components/cards/MeetupEventCard.astro';
8. import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content';
9. 
10. export async function getStaticPaths() {
11.     const groups = await getCollection('meetupGroups');
12.     return groups.map((group) => ({
13.         params: { groupSlug: group.data.slug },
14.         props: { group },
15.     }));
16. }
17. 
18. const { group } = Astro.props;
19. const { Content } = await render(group);
20. 
21. // Get all events for this group
22. let allEvents: any[] = [];
23. let upcomingEvents: any[] = [];
24. let pastEvents: any[] = [];
25. try {
26.     const allMeetupEvents = await getCollection('meetupEvents');
27.     const now = new Date();
28.     allEvents = allMeetupEvents
29.         .filter(e => e.data.meetupGroup === group.data.slug)
30.         .sort((a, b) => b.data.date.getTime() - a.data.date.getTime());
31.     
32.     upcomingEvents = allEvents.filter(e => e.data.date >= now).slice(0, 5);
33.     pastEvents = allEvents.filter(e => e.data.date < now).slice(0, 10);
34. } catch (e) {
35.     // Collection might not exist yet
36. }
37. 
38. const tabs = [
39.     { id: 'details', label: 'Meetup Details' },
40.     { id: 'events', label: `Events (${allEvents.length})` },
41. ];
42. 
43. const currentTab = Astro.url.searchParams.get('tab') || 'details';
44. 
45. const roleLabels: Record<string, string> = {
46.     'organizer': 'Organizer',
47.     'co-organizer': 'Co-Organizer',
48.     'speaker': 'Speaker',
49.     'member': 'Member',
50. };
51. ---
52. 
53. <BaseLayout 
54.     title={group.data.title}
55.     description={group.data.description}
56.     ogImage={group.data.heroImage}
57. >
58.     <section class="meetup-header">
59.         <div class="container">
60.             <div class="header-content">
61.                 <div class="meetup-logo">
62.                     <img 
63.                         src={group.data.heroImage || '/images/meetups/default-meetup.svg'} 
64.                         alt={`${group.data.title} logo`}
65.                     />
66.                 </div>
67.                 <div class="meetup-info">
68.                     {group.data.role && (
69.                         <div class="meetup-meta">
70.                             <Badge variant="primary">{group.data.role}</Badge>
71.                         </div>
72.                     )}
73.                     
74.                     <h1 class="meetup-title">{group.data.title}</h1>
75.                     
76.                     <div class="meetup-details">
77.                         <div class="detail-item">
78.                             <span class="detail-icon">📍</span>
79.                             <span class="detail-text">{group.data.city}, {group.data.state || ''} {group.data.country}</span>
80.                         </div>
81.                     </div>
82. 
83.                     <p class="meetup-description">{group.data.description}</p>
84. 
85.                     <div class="meetup-actions">
86.                         {group.data.website && (
87.                             <Button href={group.data.website} variant="primary">
88.                                 View on Meetup.com ↗
89.                             </Button>
90.                         )}
91.                     </div>
92.                 </div>
93.             </div>
94.         </div>
95.     </section>
96. 
97.     <!-- Tab Navigation -->
98.     <div class="tabs-container">
99.         <div class="container">
100.             <TabNav 
101.                 tabs={tabs.map(t => ({ ...t, href: `?tab=${t.id}` }))} 
102.                 activeTab={currentTab} 
103.             />
104.         </div>
105.     </div>
106. 
107.     <!-- Tab Content -->
108.     <section class="section tab-content">
109.         <div class="container">
110.             <!-- Details Tab -->
111.             <div class="tab-panel" id="tab-details" data-tab="details" style={currentTab !== 'details' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
112.                 <div class="meetup-content">
113.                     <Content />
114.                 </div>
115.             </div>
116. 
117.             <!-- Events Tab -->
118.             <div class="tab-panel" id="tab-events" data-tab="events" style={currentTab !== 'events' ? 'display: none;' : ''}>
119.                 {allEvents.length > 0 ? (
120.                     <div class="events-section">
121.                         {upcomingEvents.length > 0 && (
122.                             <div class="upcoming-events">
123.                                 <h3>Upcoming Events</h3>
124.                                 <div class="events-list">
125.                                     {upcomingEvents.map((event) => (
126.                                         <MeetupEventCard
127.                                             title={event.data.title}
128.                                             date={event.data.date}
129.                                             shortDescription={event.data.shortDescription || event.data.description}
130.                                             eventUrl={event.data.eventUrl}
131.                                             thumbnail={event.data.thumbnail}
132.                                             meetupGroup={group.data.slug}
133.                                             eventSlug={event.id}
134.                                         />
135.                                     ))}
136.                                 </div>
137.                             </div>
138.                         )}
139. 
140.                         {pastEvents.length > 0 && (
141.                             <div class="past-events">
142.                                 <h3>Past Events</h3>
143.                                 <div class="events-list">
144.                                     {pastEvents.map((event) => (
145.                                         <MeetupEventCard
146.                                             title={event.data.title}
147.                                             date={event.data.date}
148.                                             shortDescription={event.data.shortDescription || event.data.description}
149.                                             eventUrl={event.data.eventUrl}
150.                                             thumbnail={event.data.thumbnail}
151.                                             meetupGroup={group.data.slug}
152.                                             eventSlug={event.id}
153.                                         />
154.                                     ))}
155.                                 </div>
156.                             </div>
157.                         )}
158.                     </div>
159.                 ) : (
160.                     <div class="empty-state">
161.                         <p>No events listed yet. Check the Meetup page for the latest information.</p>
162.                         {group.data.website && (
163.                             <Button href={group.data.website} variant="primary">
164.                                 View on Meetup.com ↗
165.                             </Button>
166.                         )}
167.                     </div>
168.                 )}
169.             </div>
170.         </div>
171.     </section>
172. 
173.     <TabSyncScript />
174. 
175.     <style>
176.         .tabs-container {
177.             background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
178.             border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-border);
179.             position: sticky;
180.             top: 0;
181.             z-index: 10;
182.         }
183.

186.         }

191.             }

194.             }
195.         }

200.             gap: var(--spacing-10);
201.         }
202.

204.         .past-events {
205.             display: flex;

207.             gap: var(--spacing-4);
208.         }
209.

212.             font-size: var(--font-size-2xl);
213.             margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
214.         }
215.

219.             gap: var(--spacing-4);

221.         }
222. 
223.         .empty-state {

225.             padding: var(--spacing-10);
226.             background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
227.             border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
228.         }
229.

231.             color: var(--color-text-secondary);
232.             margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4);
233.         }
234. 
235.         .meetup-header {
236.             background-color: var(--color-bg-secondary);
237.             padding: var(--spacing-10) 0;
238.         }
239. 
240.         .header-content {
241.             display: grid;
242.             grid-template-columns: 180px 1fr;
243.             gap: var(--spacing-8);
244.             align-items: start;
245.         }
246. 
247.         .meetup-logo {
248.             background-color: var(--color-bg-primary);
249.             border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
250.             padding: var(--spacing-4);
251.             box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
252.         }

262.         }
```

```
2. import Badge from '../ui/Badge.astro';
3. import Button from '../ui/Button.astro';
4. 
5. export interface Props {
6.     title: string;
7.     description: string;
8.     slug: string;
9.     detailsHref?: string;
10.     thumbnail?: string;
11.     presentationType?: string;
12.     tags?: string[];
13.     featured?: boolean;
14.     class?: string;
15.     // Event-specific props
16.     showSchedule?: boolean;
17.     sessionName?: string;
18.     scheduleDate?: string;
19.     scheduleTime?: string;
20.     scheduleTimeZone?: string;
21.     scheduleRoom?: string;
22.     sessionUrl?: string;
23. }
24. 
25. const {
26.     title,
27.     description,
28.     slug,
29.     detailsHref,
30.     thumbnail = '/images/presentations/default-thumbnail.svg',
31.     presentationType = 'session',
32.     tags = [],
33.     featured = false,
34.     class: className = '',
35.     showSchedule = false,
36.     sessionName,
37.     scheduleDate,
38.     scheduleTime,
39.     scheduleTimeZone,
40.     scheduleRoom,
41.     sessionUrl,
42. } = Astro.props;
43. 
44. const displayTitle = sessionName || title;
45. 
46. const typeLabels: Record<string, string> = {
47.     'session': 'Session',
48.     'workshop': 'Workshop',
49.     'lightning-talk': 'Lightning Talk',
50.     'keynote': 'Keynote',
51.     'panel': 'Panel',
52.     'webinar': 'Webinar',
53. };
54. 
55. // Check if any schedule information is provided
56. const hasScheduleInfo = scheduleDate || scheduleTime || scheduleRoom;
57. const cardDetailsHref = detailsHref || `/presentations/${slug}`;
58. ---
59. 
60. <article class:list={['presentation-card', { 'featured': featured }, className]}>
61.     <a href={cardDetailsHref} class="card-image-link" aria-label={`View details for ${displayTitle}`}>
62.         <div class="card-image">
63.             <img src={thumbnail} alt="" loading="lazy" />
64.             {featured && <span class="featured-badge">Featured</span>}
65.         </div>
66.     </a>
67.     <div class="card-content">
68.         <div class="card-meta">
69.             <Badge variant="secondary">{typeLabels[presentationType] || presentationType}</Badge>
70.         </div>
71.         {showSchedule && (
72.             <div class="card-schedule">
73.                 {hasScheduleInfo ? (
74.                     <>
75.                         <div class="schedule-item">
76.                             <span class="schedule-icon">📅</span>
77.                             <span class="schedule-text">{scheduleDate || 'TBA'}</span>
78.                         </div>
79.                         <div class="schedule-item">
80.                             <span class="schedule-icon">🕐</span>
81.                             <span class="schedule-text">
82.                                 {scheduleTime || 'TBA'}{scheduleTime && scheduleTimeZone && ` ${scheduleTimeZone}`}
83.                             </span>
84.                         </div>
85.                         <div class="schedule-item">
86.                             <span class="schedule-icon">📍</span>
87.                             <span class="schedule-text">{scheduleRoom || 'TBA'}</span>
88.                         </div>
89.                     </>
90.                 ) : (
91.                     <div class="schedule-pending">
92.                         <span class="schedule-icon">📅</span>
93.                         <span class="schedule-text">Schedule pending</span>
94.                     </div>
95.                 )}
96.             </div>
97.         )}
98.         <h3 class="card-title">
99.             <a href={cardDetailsHref}>{displayTitle}</a>
100.         </h3>
101.         <p class="card-description">{description}</p>
102.         {tags.length > 0 && (
103.             <div class="card-tags">
104.                 {tags.slice(0, 3).map((tag) => (
105.                     <a href={`/presentations/tags/${encodeURIComponent(tag)}`} class="tag-link">
106.                         <Badge variant="outline" size="sm">{tag}</Badge>
107.                     </a>
108.                 ))}
109.             </div>
110.         )}
111.         <div class="card-actions">
112.             <Button href={cardDetailsHref} variant="primary" size="sm">
113.                 Details
114.             </Button>
115.             {sessionUrl && (
116.                 <Button href={sessionUrl} variant="outline" size="sm" external>
117.                     Event Page
118.                 </Button>
119.             )}
120.         </div>
121.     </div>
122. </article>
123.

243.         -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
244.         overflow: hidden;
245.     }
246. 
247.     .card-tags {
248.         display: flex;
249.         flex-wrap: wrap;
250.         gap: var(--spacing-1);
251.         margin-bottom: var(--spacing-3);

270. 
271.     .featured {

274. </style>
```

### 💬 Copilot

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary of all speaking event pages/components and how they handle upcoming vs past events:

## Summary: Speaking Events in Chad Green's Astro Site

I found **3 main pages** that display speaking events with sophisticated filtering logic for upcoming vs past events:

---

### **1. `/src/pages/speaking/index.astro` - Main Speaking Engagements Hub**

**Location:** `C:\repos\chadgreen.com/src/pages/speaking/index.astro`

**How it filters events:**
```typescript
// Lines 16-23: Date comparison using current time
const now = new Date();
const upcomingEvents = allEvents
    .filter(e =\> e.data.startDate \>= now)  // Future or today
    .sort((a, b) =\> a.data.startDate.getTime() - b.data.startDate.getTime());

const pastEvents = allEvents
    .filter(e =\> e.data.startDate \< now)  // Before today
    .sort((a, b) =\> b.data.startDate.getTime() - a.data.startDate.getTime());
```

**Key features:**
- **Upcoming Events**: Displayed as a grid of **EventCard** components, sorted chronologically (earliest first)
- **Past Events**: Grouped by **year** in descending order (newest years first), displayed as a **table** with Date, Event title, and Location columns
- Uses **UTC timezone** for all date comparisons (`timeZone: 'UTC'`)
- Shows "No upcoming events scheduled" message if empty
- Responsive table (hides Location on mobile)

**Visual distinction:**
- Upcoming: Card-based grid layout with 3-column responsive grid
- Past: Table-based layout organized by year, with year headings

---

### **2. `/src/pages/speaking/[slug].astro` - Individual Event Details Page**

**Location:** `C:\repos\chadgreen.com/src/pages/speaking/[slug].astro`

**How it filters events:**
```typescript
// Lines 89-90: Single event status determination
const now = new Date();
const isUpcoming = event.data.startDate \>= now;
```

**Key features:**
- Shows event status badges:
  - "Upcoming" badge (blue/primary) if `startDate \>= now`
  - "Past Event" badge (gray) if date is in past
- Event type badges (Conference, Meetup, Webinar, Workshop, etc.)
- Virtual event badge
- Two tabs: "About" and "My Presentations"
- Full event header with logo, dates, location, audience count

---

### **3. `/src/pages/meetups/[groupSlug]/index.astro` - Meetup Group Events**

**Location:** `C:\repos\chadgreen.com/src/pages/meetups/[groupSlug]/index.astro`

**How it filters events:**
```typescript
// Lines 27-33: Separate upcoming and past meetup events
const now = new Date();
allEvents = allMeetupEvents
    .filter(e =\> e.data.meetupGroup === group.data.slug)
    .sort((a, b) =\> b.data.date.getTime() - a.data.date.getTime());

upcomingEvents = allEvents.filter(e =\> e.data.date \>= now).slice(0, 5);
pastEvents = allEvents.filter(e =\> e.data.date \< now).slice(0, 10);
```

**Key features:**
- Shows **5 upcoming events** (limited)
- Shows **10 past events** (limited)
- Both sections use **MeetupEventCard** component for display
- Clear "Upcoming Events" and "Past Events" heading sections
- Empty state if no events

---

### **4. `/src/pages/presentations/[slug].astro` - Presentation Details (Events Tab)**

**Location:** `C:\repos\chadgreen.com/src/pages/presentations/[slug].astro`

**How it filters events:**
```typescript
// Lines 31-47: Gets all events where this presentation was given
let presentationEvents: any[] = [];
try {
    const allEvents = await getCollection('events');
    presentationEvents = allEvents
        .filter(e =\> {
            if (!e.data.presentations) return false;
            return e.data.presentations.some((p: any) =\> {
                const presentationId = typeof p === 'string' ? p : p.id;
                return presentationId === presentation.id;
            });
        })
        .sort((a, b) =\> b.data.startDate.getTime() - a.data.startDate.getTime());
} catch (e) {
    // Collection might not exist yet
}
```

**Key features:**
- Sorts by **most recent first** (no explicit upcoming/past split)
- Shows all events where the presentation was given
- Uses **EventCard** component to display each event
- EventCard itself determines if it's "upcoming" vs "completed" based on `status` prop

---

## **Event Card Components**

### **EventCard.astro** (`/src/components/cards/EventCard.astro`)

**Status Determination (Lines 81-82):**
```typescript
const isUpcoming = status === 'upcoming' || status === 'confirmed';
```

**Status Badges:**
```astro
{isUpcoming && \<span class="status-badge upcoming"\>Upcoming\</span\>}
{status === 'completed' && \<span class="status-badge completed"\>Past Event\</span\>}
```

- Blue badge for "Upcoming"
- Gray badge for "Past Event"
- Shows date range, location, event type
- Optional links to slides/demos
- Virtual event badge if applicable

### **MeetupEventCard.astro** (`/src/components/cards/MeetupEventCard.astro`)

- Simpler horizontal card layout
- Shows formatted date, title, description
- No explicit upcoming/past distinction (used in filtered lists)
- Optional thumbnail image

---

## **Key Date Comparison Pattern Used Throughout**

All pages use the **same pattern**:
```typescript
const now = new Date();
const upcomingEvents = allEvents.filter(e =\> e.data.startDate \>= now);
const pastEvents = allEvents.filter(e =\> e.data.startDate \< now);
```

**Key characteristics:**
- ✅ Comparison: `startDate \>= now` (upcoming) vs `startDate \< now` (past)
- ✅ Uses **UTC timezone** for consistency
- ✅ Includes "today" as upcoming (`\>=` not `\>`)
- ✅ All dates from content are Date objects
- ✅ Different sorting per section (upcoming: ascending, past: descending)

---

## **Content Structure**

Events are stored in `/src/content/events/` as markdown files with frontmatter:
- `startDate` (required)
- `endDate` (optional)
- `title`, `description`, `location`, `eventUrl`, `heroImage`, etc.

Presentations are in `/src/content/presentations/` and linked to events via the `presentations` field in events.


---

```
230.                                 </a>

232.                         </div>
233.                     </div>

478. <script>
479.     // Handle tab navigation without page reload
480.     const tabLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-nav a');
481.     const tabPanels = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel');
482. 
483.     tabLinks.forEach(link => {
484.         link.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
485.             e.preventDefault();
486.             const url = new URL((link as HTMLAnchorElement).href);
487.             const tabId = url.searchParams.get('tab');
488. 
489.             // Update URL without reload
490.             window.history.pushState({}, '', url);
491. 
492.             // Update active tab
493.             tabLinks.forEach(l => l.classList.remove('tab-active'));
494.             link.classList.add('tab-active');
495. 
496.             // Show corresponding panel
497.             tabPanels.forEach(panel => {
498.                 if ((panel as HTMLElement).dataset.tab === tabId) {
499.                     (panel as HTMLElement).style.display = 'block';
500.                 } else {
501.                     (panel as HTMLElement).style.display = 'none';
502.                 }
503.             });
504.         });
505.     });
506. </script>
507.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 59m 25s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Found the bug. **`EventCard` defaults `status` to `'upcoming'`**, but the events schema has no `status` field — so `event.data.status` is always `undefined`, and every card gets the `'upcoming'` default. The fix is to derive `isUpcoming` from the actual `startDate`:

---

<sub>⏱️ 59m 25s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\repos\chadgreen.com\src\pages\speaking\index.astro.**

C:\repos\chadgreen.com\src\pages\speaking\index.astro (lines 1-50)

<details>
<summary>50 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
3. import PageHeader from '../../components/ui/PageHeader.astro';
4. import EventCard from '../../components/cards/EventCard.astro';
5. import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
6. 
7. // Get all events
8. let allEvents: any[] = [];
9. try {
10.     allEvents = await getCollection('events');
11. } catch (e) {
12.     // Collection might not exist yet
13. }
14. 
15. // Separate upcoming and past events
16. const now = new Date();
17. const upcomingEvents = allEvents
18.     .filter(e => e.data.startDate >= now)
19.     .sort((a, b) => a.data.startDate.getTime() - b.data.startDate.getTime());
20. 
21. const pastEvents = allEvents
22.     .filter(e => e.data.startDate < now)
23.     .sort((a, b) => b.data.startDate.getTime() - a.data.startDate.getTime());
24. 
25. // Group past events by year
26. const pastEventsByYear = pastEvents.reduce((acc, event) => {
27.     const year = event.data.startDate.getFullYear();
28.     if (!acc[year]) acc[year] = [];
29.     acc[year].push(event);
30.     return acc;
31. }, {} as Record<number, any[]>);
32. 
33. const years = Object.keys(pastEventsByYear).sort((a, b) => Number(b) - Number(a));
34. 
35. function formatDateRange(start: Date, end?: Date): string {
36.     const utc = { timeZone: 'UTC' as const };
37.     if (!end || start.getTime() === end.getTime()) {
38.         return start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric', ...utc });
39.     }
40.     const sameMonth = start.getUTCMonth() === end.getUTCMonth() && start.getUTCFullYear() === end.getUTCFullYear();
41.     if (sameMonth) {
42.         return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.getUTCDate()}, ${end.getUTCFullYear()}`;
43.     }
44.     return `${start.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', ...utc })} - ${end.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric', ...utc })}`;
45. }
46. 
47. function formatLocation(loc: any): string {
48.     if (loc.virtual) return 'Virtual';
49.     const parts = [loc.city];
50.     if (loc.state) parts.push(loc.state);
```

<sub>⏱️ 59m 40s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The `speaking/index.astro` correctly separates events by date, but renders upcoming ones via `EventCard` without passing a `status` — so they hit the `status = 'upcoming'` default. And `presentations/[slug].astro` passes `event.data.status` which is always `undefined` (no `status` field in the events schema).

**Fix**: Compute `isUpcoming` from `startDate` in `EventCard` instead of trusting the `status` prop:

---

<sub>⏱️ 60m 11s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Fixed. The root cause was a two-part problem:

1. **`EventCard` had `status = 'upcoming'` as the default prop** — so any card that didn't explicitly pass `status` defaulted to showing "Upcoming"
2. **The events schema has no `status` field** — so `event.data.status` is always `undefined`, always hitting that default

The fix replaces the prop-based check with a direct date comparison:
```ts
const now = new Date();
const isUpcoming = startDate \>= now;
```

Past events now show **"Past Event"** and only future events show **"Upcoming"**, on both the presentation events tab and the speaking page.

---

  - Understand the different serverless, event-driven cloud services and how to put them together to build a cohesive microservice-based architecture.

  - Get hands-on experience deploying the solution you have built using continuous integration/continuous development tools that make it easy to keep your solution updated with the latest changes.

```
26. In today’s fast-evolving cloud landscape, messaging patterns are the quiet enablers behind resilient, high-performance systems. This session unlocks the secrets of these paradigms, from Point-to-Point to the innovative Saga Pattern, empowering architects and developers to design systems that scale gracefully and respond dynamically. Through hands-on implementations and vivid real-world case studies, you’ll gain technical mastery and a fresh perspective on integrating effective, future-proof messaging in your cloud solutions.
```

```
1. ---
2. title: "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution"
3. description: "A hands-on full-day workshop where you design, develop, and deploy a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using .NET, Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs."
4. type: "workshop"
5. durations: [480]
6. level: "intermediate"
7. tags:
8.   - "event-driven"
9.   - "eda"
10.   - "microservices"

14.   - "azure"

16.   - "dotnet"
17. heroImage: "/images/presentations/eda-microservices-workshop-v1.webp"
18. status: "active"
19. featured: false
20. 
21. learningObjectives:
22.   - "**Understand Serverless and Event-Driven Services**: Explore the Azure services that underpin serverless event-driven architectures—Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs—and understand how each fits into a cohesive microservice design."
23.   - "**Design a Microservice-Based Architecture**: Work through the architecture phase of a real solution, applying microservice patterns including bounded contexts, independent deployability, and loose coupling via event-driven messaging."
24.   - "**Implement Event-Driven Communication**: Build producer and consumer services that communicate asynchronously through Azure Service Bus queues/topics and Azure Event Hubs, eliminating tight inter-service dependencies."
25.   - "**Build Serverless Microservices with Azure Functions**: Develop individual microservices as Azure Functions, leveraging trigger bindings (HTTP, Service Bus, Event Hub) and output bindings to reduce boilerplate and simplify integration."
26.   - "**Apply Real-World Best Practices**: Learn proven patterns drawn from production experience—including idempotent consumers, dead-letter handling, retry policies, and saga/choreography coordination—and understand when and why to use each."
27.   - "**Develop RESTful API Layers**: Create HTTP-triggered Azure Functions that expose microservice capabilities as REST endpoints, applying proper status codes, error handling, and versioning strategies."
28.   - "**Secure Microservices and Cloud Resources**: Configure managed identities, Azure Key Vault references, and role-based access control to protect secrets and restrict access between services in the deployed solution."
29.   - "**Deploy with CI/CD Pipelines**: Set up continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines using GitHub Actions to automate building, testing, and deploying the multi-service solution to Azure."
30.   - "**Observe and Monitor Distributed Systems**: Instrument services with Application Insights, configure distributed tracing across event boundaries, and build dashboards and alerts that surface health and performance issues in a microservice landscape."
31.   - "**Optimize for Reliability and Scalability**: Configure scaling rules, consumption plan vs. premium plan trade-offs, and resilience patterns (circuit breaker, exponential backoff) so the solution meets production SLAs."
32. 
33. resources:
34.   - type: "github"
35.     title: "EDAMicroserviceWorkshop Repository"
36.     url: "https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/EDAMicroserviceWorkshop"
37.     description: "Workshop lab materials, demos, and slide assets"
38. ---
39. 
40. ## Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution
41. 
42. ### Elevator Pitch
43. 
44. Build a real serverless event-driven microservice solution from scratch in a single day—design it, code it in .NET, and ship it to Azure using CI/CD. Walk away with working code and battle-tested patterns.
45. 
46. ### Short Abstract
47. 
48. Hands-on full-day workshop where you will design, develop, and publish a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using .NET, Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs.
49. 
50. ### Full Abstract
51. 
52. You have heard all the buzzwords such as microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, etc. You probably have attended sessions that talk about these terms. But how do you put all that together?
53. 
54. During this full-day workshop, you will start by designing a solution using serverless and event-driven cloud services using microservice patterns. Then you will build that solution using .NET, Azure services, and other best-practice tools. Finally, you will deploy that solution to the cloud so your customers can reap the rewards of a well-architected, reliable, and scalable solution that meets their needs today and provides for growth in the future.
55. 
56. ### Workshop Type
57. 
58. - Full-Day Workshop (approximately 8 hours including labs)
59. 
60. ### Target Audience
61. 
62. - .NET developers looking to move beyond monoliths into event-driven microservices
63. - Software architects evaluating serverless and event-driven patterns for cloud solutions
64. - Teams adopting Azure for distributed application development
65. - Developers seeking hands-on experience with Azure Functions, Service Bus, and Event Hubs
66. 
67. ### Prerequisites
68. 
69. - Working knowledge of C# and .NET development
70. - Basic familiarity with cloud concepts (Azure account helpful but not required)
71. - Understanding of REST APIs and basic software design principles
72. - Laptop with Visual Studio or VS Code, .NET SDK, and Azure CLI installed
73. 
74. ### Delivery History
75. 
76. | Event | Location | Date |
77. |-------|----------|------|
78. | [Nebraska.Code()](https://nebraskacode.amegala.com/) | Lincoln, NE | July 17, 2024 |
79. | [CodeMash](https://codemash.org/session-details/?id=532679) | Sandusky, OH | January 10, 2024 |
80. | [TechBash](https://techbash.com/) | Pocono Manor, PA | November 7, 2023 |
81. | [Beer City Code](https://www.beercitycode.com/) | Grand Rapids, MI | August 4, 2023 |
82.
```

</details>

```
1. ---
2. title: "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution"
3. description: "A hands-on full-day workshop where you design, develop, and deploy a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using .NET, Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs."
4. type: "workshop"
5. durations: [480]
6. level: "intermediate"
7. tags:
8.   - "event-driven"
9.   - "eda"
10.   - "microservices"
11.   - "messaging"
12.   - "workshop"
13.   - "architecture"
14.   - "azure"
15.   - "serverless"
16.   - "dotnet"
17. heroImage: "/images/presentations/eda-microservices-workshop-v1.webp"
18. status: "active"
19. featured: false
20. 
21. learningObjectives:
22.   - "**Understand Serverless and Event-Driven Services**: Explore the Azure services that underpin serverless event-driven architectures—Azure Functions, Azure Service Bus, and Azure Event Hubs—and understand how each fits into a cohesive microservice design."
23.   - "**Design a Microservice-Based Architecture**: Work through the architecture phase of a real solution, applying microservice patterns including bounded contexts, independent deployability, and loose coupling via event-driven messaging."
24.   - "**Implement Event-Driven Communication**: Build producer and consumer services that communicate asynchronously through Azure Service Bus queues/topics and Azure Event Hubs, eliminating tight inter-service dependencies."
25.   - "**Build Serverless Microservices with Azure Functions**: Develop individual microservices as Azure Functions, leveraging trigger bindings (HTTP, Service Bus, Event Hub) and output bindings to reduce boilerplate and simplify integration."
26.   - "**Apply Real-World Best Practices**: Learn proven patterns drawn from production experience—including idempotent consumers, dead-letter handling, retry policies, and saga/choreography coordination—and understand when and why to use each."
27.   - "**Develop RESTful API Layers**: Create HTTP-triggered Azure Functions that expose microservice capabilities as REST endpoints, applying proper status codes, error handling, and versioning strategies."
28.   - "**Secure Microservices and Cloud Resources**: Configure managed identities, Azure Key Vault references, and role-based access control to protect secrets and restrict access between services in the deployed solution."
29.   - "**Deploy with CI/CD Pipelines**: Set up continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines using GitHub Actions to automate building, testing, and deploying the multi-service solution to Azure."
30.   - "**Observe and Monitor Distributed Systems**: Instrument services with Application Insights, configure distributed tracing across event boundaries, and build dashboards and alerts that surface health and performance issues in a microservice landscape."
31.   - "**Optimize for Reliability and Scalability**: Configure scaling rules, consumption plan vs. premium plan trade-offs, and resilience patterns (circuit breaker, exponential backoff) so the solution meets production SLAs."
32. 
33. resources:
34.   - type: "github"
35.     title: "EDAMicroserviceWorkshop Repository"
36.     url: "https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/EDAMicroserviceWorkshop"
37.     description: "Workshop lab materials, demos, and slide assets"
38. ---
```

```
# Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET

## Elevator Pitch

Master serverless solutions with Azure & .NET in a full-day workshop. Learn, build, deploy, and transform discarded appliances into magic!

## Short Abstract

Join us for a comprehensive full-day workshop on serverless computing with Azure and .NET, designed for developers and architects eager to leverage serverless technologies for creating scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions. This hands-on workshop will guide you through the fundamentals of architecting serverless applications on Azure using .NET, focusing on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. It’s not just about code and cloud but also about repurposing discarded refrigerators with eco-friendly magic and artistic flair. By the end of the day, you’ll gain practical experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure, equipping you with the skills to effectively incorporate serverless technologies into your projects. Register today to enhance your serverless development proficiency and build scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET.

## Abstract
Immerse yourself in a comprehensive full-day workshop that delves into the transformative world of serverless computing using Azure and . NET. This workshop is meticulously crafted for developers and architects keen to utilize serverless technologies' potential to construct scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions.

Throughout this intensive day, attendees will participate in practical activities illuminating the fundamental concepts, tools, and best practices for architecting serverless applications on the Azure platform using the .NET framework.

This workshop will focus on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. Imagine a frosty landscape where discarded refrigerators find new purpose, eco-friendly magic, and a touch of artistic flair. This workshop isn't just about code and cloud—it's about breathing life into appliances, one function at a time. You will build a solution for Cool Revive's remanufacturing process in the hands-on labs.

By the end of the workshop, attendees will have acquired hands-on experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure using .NET. This will equip them with the necessary skills to incorporate serverless technologies effectively into their projects.

Seize this opportunity to enhance your serverless development proficiency and elevate your applications. Register today and embark on a journey towards constructing scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET. Don't miss out!

# Type
- Full-day workshop

## Tags
- .NET
- Architecture
- Azure
- Best Practices
- Cloud Computing
- Cost Efficiency
- Developer Workshop
- Hands-On Learning
- Scalability
- Serverless Solutions

## Learning Objectives
- **Understand Key Concepts**: Gain a solid understanding of microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, and Infrastructure as Code with Terraform.
- **Learn about Azure Serverless**: Get an overview of Azure Serverless and its capabilities.
- **Understand Serverless Design Patterns**: Learn about common serverless design patterns and how to apply them in real-world scenarios.
- **Apply Knowledge to a Business Scenario**: Understand the FizzyFusion Drinks' FlavorFlow Fountain business scenario and architect a solution using the knowledge gained.
- **Hands-On Experience**: Gain practical experience by working in hands-on labs involving various systems, such as a Predictive Ingredient Replacement System, Real-Time Inventory Management System, Data Analytics Pipeline, and more.
- **Learn Best Practices**: Learn about serverless security best practices, monitoring and troubleshooting serverless applications, and scaling and performance optimization.
- **Engage in Discussions**: Participate in open discussions and Q&A to clarify doubts and share learnings.

By the end of this workshop, participants should be able to design, implement, and manage serverless applications effectively using Azure Serverless and related technologies.

## Prerequisites

Check out the [Prerequisites](prerequisites.md) page for details of attendees' expectations before the workshop.

## Presentations

| Event | Location | Date | Time | Room | Workshop Materials |
|-------|:--------:|-----:|-----:|-----:|----------:|
| [dev up 2024](https://www.devupconf.org/) | St. Charles, MO | August  14, 24024 | 8:30 am CDT | Junior Ballroom A | [Workshop Materials](EventMaterials/DevUp2024/README.md) |
| [Beer City Code](https://www.beercitycode.com/) | Grand Rapids, MI | August 2, 2024 | 9:00 am EDT | Room 255 | [Workshop Materials](https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/BuildingServerlessSolutions-BeerCityCode2024) |
| [Nebraska.Code()](https://nebraskacode.amegala.com/) [^1]  | Lincoln, NE | July 17, 2024 | 8:45 am CDT | Lancaster 2&3 | [Workshop Materials](EventMaterials/NebraskaCode2024/README.md) |

Email [chadgreen@chadgreen.com](mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com?subject=Presentation%20Request:%20Buildling%20Serverless%20Solutions%20Workshop) to have Chad present this workshop at your event.

## Resources

There are no additional resources for this presentation.

## Footnotes
[^1]: This workshop was listed as "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution."
```

```
# Workshop (Beer City Code 2024)

![Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET](thumbnail.jpg)

## Overview

Welcome to the **Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET** workshop! This full-day workshop will equip developers and architects with the knowledge and skills to create scalable, efficient, and cost-effective serverless solutions using Azure and. NET. We will explore various Azure services, serverless design patterns, and real-world scenarios throughout the day to provide hands-on experience.

## Workshop Details

- **Event**: [Beer City Code 2024](https://www.beercitycode.com/)
- **Date**: Friday, August 2, 2024
- **Duration**: Full-day
- **Location:** Room 255
- **Audience**: Anyone curious about serverless architectures, data-driven decision-making, and microservices.

## Technology and Services Covered

During this workshop, you will gain exposure to various Azure services and technologies, including Azure Functions, Azure Event Grid, Azure Logic Apps, Azure Cosmos DB, and Application Insights.

[Click here for full details.](technologies-and-services-covered.md) 

## Prerequisites

To ensure a smooth and productive learning experience, participants should install specific software tools, have active accounts and subscriptions, possess essential C# and .NET knowledge, and bring a capable laptop.

[Click here for full details.](prerequisites.md) 

## Agenda

The workshop agenda includes an introduction and overview, a session on serverless design patterns, a discussion on a business scenario, hands-on labs, additional topics such as serverless security and performance optimization, and a wrap-up with Q&A.

[Click here for full details.](agenda.md) 

## Business Scenario: Cool Revive Technologies

Cool Revive Technologies specializes in breathing new life into old refrigerators. By refurbishing and optimizing these appliances, the company contributes to sustainability, reduces waste, and provides affordable solutions for consumers. To date, Cool Revive has relied on paper-based solutions to handle their remanufacturing processes. But as they continue to grow, they need a better, more automated process to handle the overall workflow. Not only will this ease the management load of the remanufacturing process, it will improve the company's efficiency which in the end will produce even more eco-friendly products.

Read me more about the [background information](background-info/README.md) for the labs scenario.

## Slides

-  [Workshop Introduction](slides/00%20-%20Workshop%20Introduction.pdf)
-  [Introduction to Microservices: Understanding the Basics](slides/01%20-%20Prologue%20-%20Microservices.pdf)
-  [Introduction to Event-Driven Architecture: Understanding the Basics](slides/02%20-%20Prologue%20-%20Event-Driven%20Architecture.pdf)
-  [Introduction to Serverless: Beyond Traditional Infrastructure](slides/slides/03%20-%20Prologue%20-%20Serverless.pdf)
-  [Overview of Azure  Serverless: Harnessing the Power of Microservice Azure](slides/slides/04%20-%20Azure%20Serverless.pdf)
-  [Exploring Serverless Design Patterns](slides/06%20-%20Serverless%20Design%20Patterns.pdf)
-  [Lab Scenario](slides/07%20-%20Business%20Scenario.pdf)
-  [Hands On Lab Exercises](slides/slides/08%20-%20Hands-On%20Labs.pdf)
-  [Securing Serverless: Strategies and Best Practices](slides/09%20-%20Securing%20Serverless.pdf)
-  [Monitoring and  Troubleshooting  Serverless Applications: Strategies for Effective Serverless Monitoring](10%20-%20Monitoring%20Serverless.pdf)
-  [Scaling and Performance Optimization: Maximizing Efficiency in Serverless Solutions](slides/11%20-%20Scaling%20and%20Performance%20Optimization.pdf)
-  [Event-Driven Mastery: Designing Flexible and Scalable Systems](slides/11%20-%20Scaling%20and%20Performance%20Optimization.pdf)
-  [Wrap-Up](slides/13%20-%20Wrap-Up.pdf)
```

</details>

```
2. title: "The Dark Side of Design Patterns: What They Don't Tell You"
3. description: "Session details and resources for Beer City Code 2024: The Dark Side of Design Patterns: What They Don't Tell You."
4. eventSlug: "beer-city-code-2024"
5. presentationSlug: "the-dark-side-of-design-patterns"

8. date: 2024-08-03
9. time: "11:00 AM"
10. timeZone: "EDT"

14. ---
15. 
16. Thanks for joining this session at **Beer City Code 2024**.
17. 
18. ## The Dark Side of Design Patterns: What They Don't Tell You
19. 
20. Design patterns are potent tools in software development that solve recurring problems and are essential for creating maintainable, scalable, and robust systems. However, not all design patterns are created equal. Some well-respected patterns may inadvertently lead to increased complexity, decreased maintainability, and hinder productivity.
21. 
22. Join us as we explore specific design patterns that can cause more work than they are worth. Through real-world scenarios, we will uncover how these patterns can lead to unintended consequences such as convoluted code, performance bottlenecks, and intricate debugging sessions.
23. 
24. We will examine the Factory, Observer, Singleton, and Template Method patterns—examining when they add value and when they become liabilities in your codebase.
25. 
26. ### Learning Objectives
27. 
28. - Identify design patterns that may introduce more complexity than benefits.
29. - Understand the real-world implications of using certain design patterns.
30. - Learn strategies to mitigate the negative impacts of these patterns.
31. 
32.
```

</details>

```
1. ---
2. title: "Messaging Patterns to Transform Your Cloud Architecture"
3. description: "Session details and resources for dev up 2024: Messaging Patterns to Transform Your Cloud Architecture."
4. eventSlug: "devup-2024"
5. presentationSlug: "messaging-patterns-cloud-architecture"
6. sessionSlug: "messaging-patterns-cloud-architecture"
7. sessionTitle: "Messaging Patterns to Transform Your Cloud Architecture"
8. date: 2024-08-16
9. time: "TBD"
10. timeZone: "CST"
11. canonicalPath: "speaking-session"
12. ---
13. 
14. Thanks for joining this session at **dev up 2024** in St. Charles, Missouri.
15. 
16. ## Messaging Patterns to Transform Your Cloud Architecture
17. 
18. Dive deep into the core messaging paradigms that underpin resilient and scalable cloud systems. This session explores a comprehensive range of messaging patterns, including Point-to-Point, Publish-Subscribe, Message Queues, Request-Reply, Event Sourcing, and Saga Patterns that every modern architect and developer should master. You'll see how these patterns work in practice through step-by-step implementations and real-world case studies.
19. 
20. Gain actionable insights on integrating these techniques into your solutions, boosting communication effectiveness, and system reliability. Walk away with practical knowledge and a fresh set of buzzwords that will elevate your next team discussion.
21. 
22. ### Learning Objectives
23. 
24. - Master core messaging fundamentals across patterns such as publish-subscribe and request-reply to build resilient cloud architectures.
25. - Implement real-world messaging solutions through detailed demonstrations and practical case studies for production use.
26. - Transform and elevate cloud strategy with best practices for scalability, reliability, and maintainability.
27.
```

</details>

```
2. title: "Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET"

15. 
16. Thanks for joining this workshop at **Beer City Code 2024**.
17. 
18. ## Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET
19. 
20. Immerse yourself in a comprehensive full-day workshop that delves into the transformative world of serverless computing using Azure and .NET. Throughout this intensive day, you will participate in practical activities illuminating the fundamental concepts, tools, and best practices for architecting serverless applications on the Azure platform using the .NET framework.
21. 
22. This workshop focuses on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies—a company breathing new life into old refrigerators. You will build a solution for Cool Revive's remanufacturing process through hands-on labs, experiencing first-hand how serverless technologies can modernize and automate complex business workflows.
23. 
24. ### Learning Objectives
25. 
26. - Understand the key Azure serverless services and how they work together to form a cohesive, event-driven architecture.
27. - Apply serverless design patterns to architect a real-world business solution using Azure Functions, Event Grid, Service Bus, and Cosmos DB.
28. - Deploy and manage a serverless solution using CI/CD best practices, and apply security and monitoring strategies to keep it production-ready.
29.
```

</details>

```
35. 
36. Immerse yourself in a comprehensive full-day workshop that delves into the transformative world of serverless computing using Azure and .NET. This workshop is designed for developers and architects eager to leverage serverless technologies to construct scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions.
37. 
38. Throughout this intensive day, you will participate in practical activities illuminating the fundamental concepts, tools, and best practices for architecting serverless applications on the Azure platform using the .NET framework.
39. 
40. This workshop focuses on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies—a company that specializes in breathing new life into old refrigerators. By refurbishing and optimizing these appliances, they contribute to sustainability, reduce waste, and provide affordable solutions for consumers. You will build a solution for Cool Revive's remanufacturing process through hands-on labs, experiencing first-hand how serverless technologies can modernize and automate complex business workflows.
41. 
42. By the end of the workshop, you will have acquired hands-on experience designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure using .NET, equipping you with the skills to effectively incorporate serverless technologies into your own projects.
43. 
44. ### Target Audience
45. 
46. - Developers and architects eager to build scalable, cost-effective serverless solutions
47. - Teams exploring Azure Functions and serverless-first design
48. - Anyone looking for hands-on experience with Azure's event-driven and serverless services
49. 
50. ### Prerequisites
51. 
52. - Working knowledge of C# and .NET development
53. - Basic familiarity with cloud concepts (an Azure account is required for labs)
54. - Understanding of REST APIs and basic software design principles
55. - Laptop with Visual Studio or VS Code, .NET SDK, and Azure CLI installed
56.
```

</details>

```
32. 
33. You have heard all the buzzwords such as microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, etc. You probably have attended sessions that talk about these terms. But how do you put all that together?
34. 
35. During this full-day workshop, you will start by designing a solution using serverless and event-driven cloud services using microservice patterns. Then you will build that solution using .NET, Azure services, and other best-practice tools. Finally, you will deploy that solution to the cloud so your customers can reap the rewards of a well-architected, reliable, and scalable solution that meets their needs today and provides for growth in the future.
36. 
37. ### Target Audience
38. 
39. - .NET developers looking to move beyond monoliths into event-driven microservices
40. - Software architects evaluating serverless and event-driven patterns for cloud solutions
41. - Teams adopting Azure for distributed application development
42. - Developers seeking hands-on experience with Azure Functions, Service Bus, and Event Hubs
43. 
44. ### Prerequisites
45. 
46. - Working knowledge of C# and .NET development
47. - Basic familiarity with cloud concepts (Azure account helpful but not required)
48. - Understanding of REST APIs and basic software design principles
49. - Laptop with Visual Studio or VS Code, .NET SDK, and Azure CLI installed
50.
```

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 4e2e975e30c86e5d5eecdc6e50704c0b525b2320)![Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET](thumbnail.jpg)

# Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET

## Elevator Pitch

Master serverless solutions with Azure & .NET in a full-day workshop. Learn, build, deploy, and transform discarded appliances into magic!

## Short Abstract

Join us for a comprehensive full-day workshop on serverless computing with Azure and .NET, designed for developers and architects eager to leverage serverless technologies for creating scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions. This hands-on workshop will guide you through the fundamentals of architecting serverless applications on Azure using .NET, focusing on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. It’s not just about code and cloud but also about repurposing discarded refrigerators with eco-friendly magic and artistic flair. By the end of the day, you’ll gain practical experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure, equipping you with the skills to effectively incorporate serverless technologies into your projects. Register today to enhance your serverless development proficiency and build scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET.

## Abstract
Immerse yourself in a comprehensive full-day workshop that delves into the transformative world of serverless computing using Azure and . NET. This workshop is meticulously crafted for developers and architects keen to utilize serverless technologies' potential to construct scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions.

Throughout this intensive day, attendees will participate in practical activities illuminating the fundamental concepts, tools, and best practices for architecting serverless applications on the Azure platform using the .NET framework.

This workshop will focus on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. Imagine a frosty landscape where discarded refrigerators find new purpose, eco-friendly magic, and a touch of artistic flair. This workshop isn't just about code and cloud—it's about breathing life into appliances, one function at a time. You will build a solution for Cool Revive's remanufacturing process in the hands-on labs.

By the end of the workshop, attendees will have acquired hands-on experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure using .NET. This will equip them with the necessary skills to incorporate serverless technologies effectively into their projects.

Seize this opportunity to enhance your serverless development proficiency and elevate your applications. Register today and embark on a journey towards constructing scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET. Don't miss out!

# Type
- Full-day workshop

## Tags
- .NET
- Architecture
- Azure
- Best Practices
- Cloud Computing
- Cost Efficiency
- Developer Workshop
- Hands-On Learning
- Scalability
- Serverless Solutions

## Learning Objectives
- **Understand Key Concepts**: Gain a solid understanding of microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, and Infrastructure as Code with Terraform.
- **Learn about Azure Serverless**: Get an overview of Azure Serverless and its capabilities.
- **Understand Serverless Design Patterns**: Learn about common serverless design patterns and how to apply them in real-world scenarios.
- **Apply Knowledge to a Business Scenario**: Understand the FizzyFusion Drinks' FlavorFlow Fountain business scenario and architect a solution using the knowledge gained.
- **Hands-On Experience**: Gain practical experience by working in hands-on labs involving various systems, such as a Predictive Ingredient Replacement System, Real-Time Inventory Management System, Data Analytics Pipeline, and more.
- **Learn Best Practices**: Learn about serverless security best practices, monitoring and troubleshooting serverless applications, and scaling and performance optimization.
- **Engage in Discussions**: Participate in open discussions and Q&A to clarify doubts and share learnings.

By the end of this workshop, participants should be able to design, implement, and manage serverless applications effectively using Azure Serverless and related technologies.

## Prerequisites

Check out the [Prerequisites](prerequisites.md) page for details of attendees' expectations before the workshop.

## Presentations

| Event | Location | Date | Time | Room | Workshop Materials |
|-------|:--------:|-----:|-----:|-----:|----------:|
| [dev up 2024](https://www.devupconf.org/) | St. Charles, MO | August  14, 24024 | 8:30 am CDT | Junior Ballroom A | [Workshop Materials](EventMaterials/DevUp2024/README.md) |
| [Beer City Code](https://www.beercitycode.com/) | Grand Rapids, MI | August 2, 2024 | 9:00 am EDT | Room 255 | [Workshop Materials](https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/BuildingServerlessSolutions-BeerCityCode2024) |
| [Nebraska.Code()](https://nebraskacode.amegala.com/) [^1]  | Lincoln, NE | July 17, 2024 | 8:45 am CDT | Lancaster 2&3 | [Workshop Materials](EventMaterials/NebraskaCode2024/README.md) |

Email [chadgreen@chadgreen.com](mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com?subject=Presentation%20Request:%20Buildling%20Serverless%20Solutions%20Workshop) to have Chad present this workshop at your event.

## Resources

There are no additional resources for this presentation.

## Footnotes
[^1]: This workshop was listed as "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution."
```

```
# Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution (Workshop)

## Abstract
You have heard all the buzzwords such as microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, etc. You probably have attended sessions that talk about these terms. But how do you put all that together?

During this full-day workshop, you will start by designing a solution using serverless and event-driven cloud services using microservice patterns. Then you will build that solution using .NET, Azure services, and other best-practice tools. Finally, you will deploy that solution to the cloud so your customers can reap the rewards of a well-architected, reliable, and scalable solution that meets their needs today and provides for growth in the future.

## Short Abstract
Hands-on full-day workshop where you will design, develop, and publish a serverless event-driven microservice-based solution.

# Type
* Full-day workshop

## Tags
-	.NET
-	APIs
-	Application Development
-	Architecture
-	Azure
-	Azure Event Hubs
-	Azure Functions
-	Azure Service Bus
-	C#
-	Cloud
-	Cloud Architecture
-	Cloud Computing
-	Cloud Development
-	Cloud Integration
-	Cloud Resources
-	Cloud Services
-	Cloud Solutions
-	Cloud-Native
-	Cloud-Native Services
-	Event Processing
-	Event-driven
-	Event-Driven Architecture
-	Event-Driven Solutions
-	Hands-on Training
-	Hands-on Workshop
-	Microservices
-	Microsoft
-	Microsoft Azure
-	Microservices
-	Performance Optimization
-	Real-Time Event Processing
-	Reliability
-	REST
-	Scalability
-	Scalable Architectures
-	Security
-	Serverless
-	Serverless Computing
-	Software Design
-	Software Development
-	Technology
-	Training
-	Workshop

## Learning Objectives
* Understand the different serverless, event-driven cloud services and how to put them together to build a cohesive microservice-based architecture
* Build a working serverless event-driven microservice-based solution using best practices based upon real-world experience of what has worked and what has not
* Get hands-on experience deploying the solution you have built using continuous integration/continuous development tools that make it easy to keep your solution updated with the latest changes
```

```
# Building Serverless Solutions with Azure and .NET

## Elevator Pitch

Master serverless solutions with Azure & .NET in a full-day workshop. Learn, build, deploy, and transform discarded appliances into magic!

## Short Abstract

Join us for a comprehensive full-day workshop on serverless computing with Azure and .NET, designed for developers and architects eager to leverage serverless technologies for creating scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions. This hands-on workshop will guide you through the fundamentals of architecting serverless applications on Azure using .NET, focusing on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. It’s not just about code and cloud but also about repurposing discarded refrigerators with eco-friendly magic and artistic flair. By the end of the day, you’ll gain practical experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure, equipping you with the skills to effectively incorporate serverless technologies into your projects. Register today to enhance your serverless development proficiency and build scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET.

## Abstract
Immerse yourself in a comprehensive full-day workshop that delves into the transformative world of serverless computing using Azure and . NET. This workshop is meticulously crafted for developers and architects keen to utilize serverless technologies' potential to construct scalable, efficient, and cost-effective solutions.

Throughout this intensive day, attendees will participate in practical activities illuminating the fundamental concepts, tools, and best practices for architecting serverless applications on the Azure platform using the .NET framework.

This workshop will focus on building a real-world business scenario for Cool Revive Technologies. Imagine a frosty landscape where discarded refrigerators find new purpose, eco-friendly magic, and a touch of artistic flair. This workshop isn't just about code and cloud—it's about breathing life into appliances, one function at a time. You will build a solution for Cool Revive's remanufacturing process in the hands-on labs.

By the end of the workshop, attendees will have acquired hands-on experience in designing, developing, and deploying serverless solutions on Azure using .NET. This will equip them with the necessary skills to incorporate serverless technologies effectively into their projects.

Seize this opportunity to enhance your serverless development proficiency and elevate your applications. Register today and embark on a journey towards constructing scalable, resilient, and cost-efficient solutions with Azure and .NET. Don't miss out!

# Type
- Full-day workshop

## Tags
- .NET
- Architecture
- Azure
- Best Practices
- Cloud Computing
- Cost Efficiency
- Developer Workshop
- Hands-On Learning
- Scalability
- Serverless Solutions

## Learning Objectives
- **Understand Key Concepts**: Gain a solid understanding of microservices, event-driven architecture, serverless, and Infrastructure as Code with Terraform.
- **Learn about Azure Serverless**: Get an overview of Azure Serverless and its capabilities.
- **Understand Serverless Design Patterns**: Learn about common serverless design patterns and how to apply them in real-world scenarios.
- **Apply Knowledge to a Business Scenario**: Understand the FizzyFusion Drinks' FlavorFlow Fountain business scenario and architect a solution using the knowledge gained.
- **Hands-On Experience**: Gain practical experience by working in hands-on labs involving various systems, such as a Predictive Ingredient Replacement System, Real-Time Inventory Management System, Data Analytics Pipeline, and more.
- **Learn Best Practices**: Learn about serverless security best practices, monitoring and troubleshooting serverless applications, and scaling and performance optimization.
- **Engage in Discussions**: Participate in open discussions and Q&A to clarify doubts and share learnings.

By the end of this workshop, participants should be able to design, implement, and manage serverless applications effectively using Azure Serverless and related technologies.

## Prerequisites

Check out the [Prerequisites](prerequisites.md) page for details of attendees' expectations before the workshop.

## Presentations

| Event | Location | Date | Time | Room | Workshop Materials |
|-------|:--------:|-----:|-----:|-----:|----------:|
| [dev up 2024](https://www.devupconf.org/) | St. Charles, MO | August  14, 24024 | 8:30 am CDT | Junior Ballroom A | [Workshop Materials](https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/BuildingServerlessSolutions-DevUp2024) |
| [Beer City Code](https://www.beercitycode.com/) | Grand Rapids, MI | August 2, 2024 | 9:00 am EDT | Room 255 | [Workshop Materials](https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/BuildingServerlessSolutions-BeerCityCode2024) |
| [Nebraska.Code()](https://nebraskacode.amegala.com/) [^1]  | Lincoln, NE | July 17, 2024 | 8:45 am CDT | Lancaster 2&3 | [Workshop Materials](EventMaterials/NebraskaCode2024/README.md) |

Email [chadgreen@chadgreen.com](mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com?subject=Presentation%20Request:%20Buildling%20Serverless%20Solutions%20Workshop) to have Chad present this workshop at your event.

## Resources

There are no additional resources for this presentation.

## Footnotes
[^1]: This workshop was listed as "Design and Develop a Serverless Event-Driven Microservice-Based Solution."
```

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: c9695d8aa51ff68ad60e023fe404915e92b2428b)![Building Resilient and Scalable APIs in Azure](Thumbnail.jpg)

## Short Abstract
Resilient APIs fuel scalable applications. Learn how Azure API Management, Functions, and Azure Container Apps work together to create reliable, high-performing API architectures.

## Abstract
Modern applications demand APIs that can withstand failures and dynamically scale to meet demand. In this session, we’ll explore how Azure API Management, Azure Functions, and Azure Container Apps can help design resilient and scalable APIs that remain operational under pressure.

- High Availability & Disaster Recovery: Architecting APIs to ensure seamless failover and minimal downtime.
- Resilience Strategies: To enhance reliability, implement retry policies, circuit breakers, and throttling.
- Monitoring & Observability: Using Azure Monitor and Application Insights for real-time performance tracking.
- Live Demo & Real-World Scenarios: Watch Azure tools in action as we integrate resilience techniques into a scalable API framework.

Whether you’re a developer, architect, or IT professional, this session provides actionable strategies for future-proofing your APIs and optimizing their reliability in cloud-native environments.

# Type
- 45/60/75-minute session

## Tags
![API Architecture](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tag-APIArchitecture-blue) ![Cloud-Native Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tag-CloudNativeDevelopment-blue) ![Observability](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tag-Observability-blue) ![Performance Optimization](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tag-PerformanceOptimization-blue)

## Learning Objectives
- **Resilient API Design:** Ensuring high availability & fault tolerance.
- **Scalability Techniques:** Using Azure services to scale APIs dynamically.
- **Observability Best Practices:** Monitoring APIs effectively for performance & security.

## Presentations

| Event | Location | Date | Time | Room | Downloads |
|-------|:--------:|-----:|-----:|-----:|----------:|
| [API Conference Berlin 2025](https://apiconference.net/berlin/) (2) | Berlin, Germany | October 21, 2025 | 15:45 CET | Salon 2 | [Slides](EventMaterials\BuidlingResilientAndScalbleAPIsInAzure-APIBerlin2025.pdf) |
| [API Conference New York 2025](https://apiconference.net/new-york/)  (1) | New York, NY | September 29 - October 2, 2025 | 16:00 EDT | Coney Island | [Slides](EventMaterials\BuildingResilientAndScalableAPIsInAzure-APINewYork.pdf) |

(1) Listed as Scaling APIs Like a City That Never Sleeps: Building Resilient APIs in Azure  
(2) Listed as Scaling APIs Like the Heart of Berlin: Building Resilient APIs in Azure

Email [chadgreen@chadgreen.com](mailto:chadgreen@chadgreen.com?subject=Presentation%20Request:%20Presentation%20Title) to have Chad present this session at your event.
```

</details>

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 96d3a991fb721bdcc6a67bcbe3300fb9e2095356)![Developing a Resilient Serverless Solution](Thumbnail.jpg)

## Abstract
So, you have decided to develop a serverless solution using Azure Functions because it makes it easy to get things up and running and handles all the scalability issues for you. All you have to do is write the code. However, while Azure Functions (and other similar serverless services) offer a great set of features that make scalability, resilience, and reliability easier, it is not fool-proof and can still go down. During this session, we will talk about how to make your Azure Function solutions more resilient, how to validate it will continue to work under load, and how to know about potential failures or at least the failures before your customer does.

## Short Abstract
Just because serverless means you do not have to worry about the server less, there is still work needed to ensure resiliency.

# Type
* 45/60/75-minute session

## Tags
* .NET
* Architecture
* Azure
* Azure Functions
* Best Practices
* C#
* Cloud
* Cloud-Native
* Software Design
* Microservices
* Microsoft
* Serverless

## Learning Objectives
* Learn how to not just build Azure Function solutions but Azure Function solutions that take extra steps for resiliency
* See quality techniques for testing your Azure Functions to ensure that they work under load
* Learn how to monitor your Azure Functions to know about failures as soon as possible

## Presentations

| Event | Location | Date | Time | Room | Downloads |
|-------|:--------:|-----:|-----:|-----:|----------:|
This session has not been scheduled yet
```

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 08eb6b80a926976a64de5b2c4a4a76c26cb84c71)# Going Schema-less: How to migrate a relational database to a NoSQL database

![Going Schema-less: How to migrate a relational database to a NoSQL database](thumbnail.jpg)

## Elevator Pitch

Discover the advantages of NoSQL over relational databases and learn migration strategies, including leveraging multiple data stores.

## Short Abstract

This session dives into the transition from relational to NoSQL databases, highlighting NoSQL's advantages, such as scalability and flexibility. Attendees will learn practical strategies for migrating data, including the secret of leveraging multiple data stores. Join us for actionable insights and real-world examples to guide your database evolution.

## Abstract
In the ever-evolving landscape of database management, the debate between relational and NoSQL databases remains ongoing. While relational databases have long been the stalwarts of structured data management, NoSQL databases offer a compelling alternative with their schema-less design and horizontal scalability. This presentation dives into migrating from a relational database to a NoSQL counterpart, exploring the benefits and challenges of such a transition.

We will unravel the intricacies of NoSQL databases, highlighting their flexibility and agility in handling diverse data structures. Through real-world examples and case studies, we will illustrate the tangible advantages that NoSQL databases offer over their relational counterparts, including improved scalability, performance, and schema flexibility.

Moreover, we will uncover the secret to successful migration: the strategic data allocation across multiple data sources. By embracing a polyglot persistence approach, organizations can leverage the strength of different database technologies to optimize data storage and retrieval.

Attendees will gain actionable insights into the migration process, from initial assessment and planning to execution and optimization. Whether contemplating a migration or seeking to optimize your existing database architecture, this session equips you with the knowledge and strategies to navigate the transition to a schema-less paradigm. Join us as we journey towards a more agile and scalable data ecosystem.



# Type
- 45/60/75-minute session

## Tags
- .NET
- .NET Core
- Architecture
- Azure
- C#
- Cloud
- Cosmos DB
- Data
- Data Modeling
- Database
- Database Development
- NoSQL
- SQL Server

## Learning Objectives
- Learn about the pros and cons of developing a NoSQL data model over a relational one.
- Learn how to migrate data from a relational database to a NoSQL data model.
- Learn how to develop a .NET application to use a Cosmos DB NoSQL data model

## Presentations
```
